repositories.brief_summary: Active week with typechecker improvements, stdlib additions, runtime fixes, and ongoing ephemeron GC refactoring work.
repositories.new_features_summary: New Format.pp_print_dynarray function added, nested with-constraints in package types supported, and Atomic.update merged.
repositories.new_features:
- **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** added to the standard library for printing `Dynarray.t` values with a separator, mirroring `pp_print_array` and `pp_print_iarray` [#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726)
- **Nested `with` constraints in package types** are now supported as syntactic sugar: `(module T with type x = ... with type y = ...)` is accepted alongside the existing `and` form [#14274](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14274) [#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14710)
- **`Atomic.update`** (formerly `Atomic.modify`) merged, providing a high-level retry-loop abstraction over `compare_and_set` [#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798)
- **`ctype.mli`** exposes new `filter_arity` function and `Pattern_env.save`/`reset` state for external use [#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738)
- **`errortrace.mli`** gains a new `Kind_differ` error variant for better reporting of mismatched privacy levels on object method types [#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738)
repositories.activity_summary: Twelve PRs merged this week covering typechecker correctness, memory optimizations, runtime bug fixes, and build system improvements.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened [#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754) to fix cyclic types introduced by `with type` module constraints by rechecking well-foundedness after applying them
- **Newly opened**: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened [#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746) to allow aliases when checking signature inclusion, fixing [#14204](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14204) with careful handling of private types and parameter reordering
- **Newly opened**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [#14759](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14759) to add C stubs for testing `OCAMLRUNPARAM` parsing by calling `caml_parse_ocamlrunparam` directly
- **Newly opened**: [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan) opened [#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757) to add explicit unit suffixes (bytes/words) to memory-size OCAMLRUNPARAM options
- **Newly opened**: [Yihang "Corvo" Liu](https://github.com/glyh) opened [#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756) adding `kw/Mw/Gw`, `kB/MB/GB`, and `kiB/MiB/GiB` suffixes to OCAMLRUNPARAM word-sized params, closing [#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658)
- **Newly opened**: [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan) opened [#14755](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14755) to fix a broken link to `ocamltest/OCAMLTEST.adoc` in `testsuite/HACKING.adoc`
- **Newly opened**: [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) opened issue [#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758) reporting an internal error in `select_win32.c` related to cross-list socket merging corrupting `lpOrigIdx`
- **Merged**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged [#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721) fixing a spurious syntactic arity error for module-dependent arrow types, closing [#14719](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14719)
- **Merged**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged [#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14710) adding nested `with` constraints in package types, closing [#14274](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14274)
- **Merged**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged [#14727](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14727) reducing typechecker memory use during counter-example search in exhaustiveness checking by scoping type node pools to individual branches
- **Merged**: [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) merged [#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738) simplifying field-kind handling in `eqtype` and `moregen` and [#14737](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14737) unifying the order of missing-field checks between the two functions
- **Merged**: [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) merged [#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666) enabling `-Wwrite-strings` throughout the C runtime, followed by [#14751](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14751) fixing a Cygwin build error the change exposed
- **Merged**: [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) merged [#14748](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14748) fixing an uninitialized `malloc` in `ocamlyacc`'s `symtab.c` that could produce incorrect line directives, fixing [#14747](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14747)
- **Merged**: [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) merged [#14691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14691) adding overflow checking to `caml_ba_reshape` to fix an out-of-bounds read / segfault on 4.14, fixing [#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655) and [#14674](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14674)
- **Merged**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) merged [#14749](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14749) hardening `ocamltest` to reject expect tests that use `include` and fixing AFL test noise
- **Merged**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) merged [#14742](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14742) promoting a Cmm output test whose stamps changed after [#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798)
- **Merged**: [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) merged [#14723](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14723) with minor cleanup in `Typecore.type_effect_cases`
- **Merged**: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged [#14703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14703) fixing swapped `None`/`Some` branches in `bytecomp/dll.ml` `open_dll` when upgrading a DLL from checking to execution mode
- **Merged**: [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) merged [#14730](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14730) adding a regression test for the `rectypes` keep-expansion fix of [#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648)
- **Merged**: [Sai Asish Y](https://github.com/SAY-5) merged [#14752](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14752) fixing the `occured` → `occurred` typo in three `ocamldoc` files
- **Closed without merge**: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) closed [#14736](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14736) (follow type abbreviations for manifest types) after discovering it incorrectly accepted parameter-reordered types; superseded by [#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)
- **Closed without merge**: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) closed draft [#14712](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14712) (all-in-one rectypes expect test variant) after incorporating its approach into [#14643](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14643)
repositories.discussions_summary: Ongoing debate around OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffixes and a discussion about memory usage patterns for ocamlyacc's malloc vs calloc usage.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffix design** attracted discussion between [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan), and [Yihang "Corvo" Liu](https://github.com/glyh) about whether to support `MiB`/`MB`/`Mw` or only `MiB`-style IEC prefixes to avoid SI vs binary confusion; [#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757) [#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756) [#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658)
- **ocamlyacc `calloc` vs `malloc`** prompted a philosophical exchange between [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) about whether zero-initialization by default hides bugs or reduces CVE risk in this legacy codebase [#14748](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14748)
- **Merging `eqtype` and `moregen`** continued with [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) landing two preparatory PRs and opening [#14739](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14739) for the final merge, with [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) noting subtle differences in variable handling [#14364](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14364)
- **PR [#14752](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14752) typo-fix policy** sparked debate about accepting mass-typo PRs from contributors optimising GitHub metrics, with [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) expressing concern and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ultimately merging and closing the discussion
repositories.trends_summary: Sustained investment in ephemeron GC correctness and a push to improve type error diagnostics across eqtype, moregen, and object types.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron GC refactoring** is a multi-PR campaign by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) building on [#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720): [#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) refactors phase-change helpers, [#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) fixes orphaning during sweep phase, [#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) clarifies ephemeron counters, and [#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735) introduces higher-level list operations
- **C compiler warnings cleanup** is an ongoing effort by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): `-Wwrite-strings` landed this week [#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666) and `-Wformat-signedness` [#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694) is open for review
- **Typechecker diagnostics improvements** feature prominently: object-type privacy errors [#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738), signature-inclusion alias errors [#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746), and arity error fixes [#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721) all landed or are in review
- **`Atomic` stdlib expansion**: `Atomic.update` merged [#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798) and `Atomic.fold_update` [#14725](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14725) is proposed as a follow-up to support pop-style operations on lock-free data structures
repositories.issues_summary: One good-first-issue attracted multiple PRs this week around adding explicit unit suffixes to OCAMLRUNPARAM memory parameters.
repositories.good_first_issues: - **[#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658)** — `OCAMLRUNPARAM` memory-size parameters (e.g. `s`, `l`, `e`) currently accept only word-counted multipliers (`k`, `M`, `G`), making it easy to misinterpret `s=2M` as 2 MiB rather than 16 MiB; the issue requests adding byte and word suffix variants; attracted [#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756) and [#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757) as competing implementations

repositories.brief_summary: Week 16 of 2026 was dominated by the release of opam 2.5.1, a security and bug-fix release. The week saw 38 commits spanning security hardening, GC corruption fixes, OS detection improvements, build system cleanup, and post-release follow-up work. A security fix addressed a path-escape vulnerability in .install files (OSEC-2026-03), and two rare GC corruption bugs in C stubs were resolved.
repositories.new_features_summary: The main new features were post-release infrastructure improvements: Nix depexts now loads package names from a JSON file instead of inlining them, the build system gained a helper script for platforms without autoconf 2.71, and a performance-focused PR to remove build directories early during package installation was opened.
repositories.new_features:
- [#6894](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6894) Nix depexts now loads package names from a JSON file instead of inlining them, fixing a potential string injection via the depexts field to nix-build when `os-family=nixos` — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906) Opened PR to remove the build directory as soon as possible when installing a package, addressing performance issue [#5884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5884) — by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6905) GHA depexts CI improvements: speeds up testing by not recompiling OCaml each time, keeps distributions up-to-date, and puts rolling-release and version-based distributions on equal footing — opened by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6878](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6878) Dependency lower-bounds spring cleanup: added lower bounds for `ocamlgraph`, `jsonm`, `swhid_core`, `uutf`; required `spdx_licenses` >= 1.4.0 for SPDX v3 compatibility; added a helper script for generating configure on platforms without autoconf 2.71 — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
repositories.activity_summary: The week centred on the opam 2.5.1 release and its immediate follow-up. Seventeen PRs were merged, covering a security fix for .install path escapes, two GC corruption bug fixes in C stubs, Gentoo detection restoration, and various CI and build system improvements. The minimum OCaml version for building opam was raised from 4.08 to 4.11.
repositories.activity:
- [#6899](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6899) Released opam 2.5.1, a security and bug-fix release — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897) Security fix (OSEC-2026-03): invalidate .install fields containing destination filepaths that attempt to escape their scope using `..` or absolute paths — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), with test improvements by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) Fixed rare GC corruption in `OpamStubs.uname` by registering roots for intermediate strings — merged from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), reported by [Andrew Nesbitt](https://github.com/andrew)
- [#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882) Fixed rare GC corruption in `OpamStubs.enumRegistry` on Windows — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6886](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6886) Restored Gentoo distribution detection and added support for single-quoted values in `/etc/os-release` — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), fixing [#6887](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6887)
- [#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879) Removed support for building opam with OCaml 4.08, 4.09, and 4.10; minimum is now 4.11 — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6885) Renamed `result` type to `solver_result` in `opamTypes` to avoid conflict with `Stdlib.result` — merged by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- [#6902](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6902) Added 2.5.1 binaries to install.sh — merged by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- [#6903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6903) Fixed the release script not being a no-op on re-runs — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6904](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6904) Post-2.5.1 release bumps: ported changelog, updated documentation and GHA to 2.5.1 — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6889](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6889) Sped up macOS CI builds by stopping testing alternative solvers on macOS — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6888](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6888) Disabled testing `conf-clang-format` in favour of `conf-fts` on Alpine in CI — merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883) Opened discussion on standardising C stub allocation patterns to prevent future GC corruptions, with review feedback from [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
repositories.discussions_summary: The week featured active discussion around the opam 2.5.1 release's impact on downstream tooling, particularly setup-ocaml, and requests for improved release security practices.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#6901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6901) [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) reported that setup-ocaml broke when opam 2.5.1 released without pre-built binaries immediately; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) clarified binaries are for the install script, not setup-ocaml, redirecting to the setup-ocaml issue tracker; [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) linked the corresponding setup-ocaml fix
- [#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900) [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) requested switching to GitHub immutable releases to harden against supply chain attacks; [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) suggested combining this with GitHub artifact attestations
- [#6907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6907) [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) raised the issue that `opam-installer` has a dependency cycle via `opam-core` depending on `jsonm`, making it hard to install packages like `jsonm` without opam
- [#6884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6884) [Pierre Villemot](https://github.com/Halbaroth) reported that `opam pin edit` and `opam show --raw` display a reformatted rather than raw opam file; investigation revealed the file contains a parse error that opam silently recovers from
- [#6881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6881) [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares) asked about using opam to install Cygwin packages in CI after `setup-x86_64.exe` stopped working; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) suggested `opam init --reinit --cygwin-extra-packages=...`
repositories.trends_summary: The week highlights ongoing efforts to improve opam's security posture, eliminate GC corruption hazards in C stubs, modernise the minimum OCaml version support, and strengthen the release infrastructure. The introduction of structured C stub allocation standards reflects growing attention to safety in the runtime interface.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Security hardening focus: the OSEC-2026-03 fix for .install path escape and the ongoing discussion on C stub GC root registration [#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883) reflect a sustained effort to eliminate classes of vulnerabilities
- GC safety in C stubs: fixes to `uname` [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) and `enumRegistry` [#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882) and the proposed standardisation [#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883) show active work on ensuring all C bindings correctly register OCaml GC roots
- Minimum OCaml version bump: dropping support for OCaml 4.08–4.10 [#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879) and removing compat shims continues the trend of reducing technical debt
- Supply chain security concerns from downstream users [#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900) signal growing ecosystem interest in attestations and immutable releases
- Nushell support [#6873](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6873) remains open, reflecting continued demand for shell environment integration beyond bash/zsh/fish
repositories.contributors_summary: The week's activity was heavily led by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) who drove the 2.5.1 release and the majority of bug fixes and CI work. [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) contributed the install script update and the solver type rename. [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) contributed the uname GC fix. [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) had the Nix string injection fix included in the release.
repositories.contributors:

- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) — added 2.5.1 binaries to install.sh [#6902](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6902), contributed tests for .install path escape fix, renamed `result` to `solver_result` [#6885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6885)
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) — fixed GC corruption in `OpamStubs.uname` [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880)
- [Ryan Gibb](https://github.com/RyanGibb) — authored the Nix string injection fix included in 2.5.1 via [#6894](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6894)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) — provided review guidance on C stub allocation standardisation in [#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883)
- [Andrew Nesbitt](https://github.com/andrew) — reported the GC corruption bugs in uname and enumRegistry that led to [#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880) and [#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882)

repositories.brief_summary: Test fix merged, version bumped toward 5.7.0, and new PRs opened for menhirLib, misc.ml sync, and saved parts investigation.
repositories.activity_summary: Four new PRs opened covering menhirLib snapshot update, misc.ml compiler sync, saved parts testing, and a CI dependency bump; one test fix merged.
repositories.activity:
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058) to **update menhirLib's snapshotting mechanism**, switching to the `--dump-menhirLib` flag introduced in 2023 after noticing copy rules were failing with recent (2026) menhir versions; a build refresh is needed before merging
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) as an **investigation into Merlin's saved parts handling**, motivated by review of [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241); the PR adds tests illustrating a **browse tree blowup** that can occur when mimicking the typechecker's behavior for saving types on success
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened [#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) to **synchronize `misc.ml` with the upstream OCaml compiler**, moving `src/utils/misc.ml` to `src/ocaml/utils/misc.ml` and repairing accumulated divergence; also moves `format_doc.ml` to align with compiler structure across 30 changed files
- [Dependabot](https://github.com/dependabot) opened [#2056](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2056) to **bump `cachix/install-nix-action`** from 31.8.4 to 31.10.4, including a security fix for a root privilege escalation vulnerability (GHSA-g3g9-5vj6-r3gj); the previous superseded bump PR [#2052](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2052) was closed without merge
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) **fixed jq array indexing syntax** in `issue1949.t` test (`.value.[0]` → `.value[0]`) for compatibility with jq 1.6, merged as [#2057](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2057) by [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos)
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) pushed a direct commit ([097d8ba](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/commit/097d8ba093c7b55097a6f9c8ac7def58196ef3fb)) to **update the version constraint** in the ocaml-lsp compatibility CI check from `5.6-504` to `5.7.0-504`, suggesting preparation for an upcoming Merlin 5.7.0 release
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) has an ongoing PR [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051) (opened March 24, updated this week) adding **support for pointing indexes in ocaml-index**, enabling smaller index files and fewer write operations; review discussion with [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) addressed ID comparison and filename checks, with tests added in `union.t`
repositories.discussions_summary: Saved parts PR sparked discussion on browse tree blowup risks and the need for a tracking ticket for future compiler updates.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In [#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060), [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) notes that Merlin's handling of **saved types without type errors** differs from the typechecker, and that mimicking the typechecker leads to unwanted nesting and browse tree blowup (demonstrated in commit `95ba06e`); [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) suggested opening a ticket to track the issue going forward
- The discussion also highlights that the blowup is unlikely to cause visible behavior differences in most Merlin commands (which deduplicate nodes by location) but could have **performance implications** that remain uncertain
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to align Merlin's internals with the upstream OCaml compiler, both in shared utilities and parser infrastructure.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Multiple PRs this week reflect a push toward **closer synchronization with the upstream OCaml compiler**: [#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) syncs `misc.ml` and `format_doc.ml` to reduce historical drift, while [#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058) modernizes the menhirLib snapshot mechanism
- The **saved parts investigation** in [#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) suggests growing attention to correctness and performance of Merlin's internal typedtree handling, particularly in anticipation of ongoing OCaml compiler changes ([ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241))
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors active this week: Liam Stevenson with a merged test fix and new misc.ml sync PR, Thomas Refis with two new investigative PRs, and Ulysse with a CI version bump commit.
repositories.contributors:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merged [#2057](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2057) fixing jq array indexing in test infrastructure, and opened [#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) for the substantial `misc.ml` synchronization work
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058) for the menhirLib snapshot update and [#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) as a test-and-investigation PR for saved parts behavior, including commentary on browse tree blowup risks
- [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) reviewed and merged [#2057](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2057) with a thank-you comment, and pushed a direct commit updating the LSP compatibility version constraint to `5.7.0-504`
- [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) continued work on [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051), incorporating review feedback from [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) on index ID comparison and filename validation
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) provided code review on [#2051](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2051), suggesting filename comparisons alongside ID checks and requesting additional aggregation tests
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) commented on [#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) suggesting a tracking ticket be opened for the saved parts / browse tree blowup issue

repositories.brief_summary: A busy week ahead of the 3.23.0 release with compile_commands.json generation, SIGTERM grace period for child processes, opam file workflow changes, and extensive RPC and internal refactoring.
repositories.new_features_summary: Key new features include compile_commands.json generation for C/C++ editor support, graceful SIGTERM-before-SIGKILL shutdown, RPC lifecycle trace events, and a new trace flag syntax.
repositories.new_features:
- **compile_commands.json generation** for C/C++ foreign stubs is now produced as part of `@check` when using `(lang dune 3.23)`, enabling clangd, ccls, and other tools that consume compilation databases ([#14185](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14185), by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- **SIGTERM before SIGKILL** is now sent to child processes both on shutdown and on watch-mode cancellation, giving cleanup handlers a chance to run before escalating to SIGKILL — fixes a 7-year-old issue ([#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14224), [#14170](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14170), fixes [#2445](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/2445), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **Opam file generation now requires explicit promotion**: files must be manually promoted with `dune promote` rather than being auto-promoted; generation is triggered by `@install`, `@runtest`, and `@opam`; in release mode the source opam files are used directly ([#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)) — **requires dune 3.23**
- **RPC lifecycle trace events** added for accepting clients and shutting down the RPC server, improving observability ([#14232](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14232), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Tiny syntax for trace flags** added, allowing users to easily add or remove individual trace flags ([#14230](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14230), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Watch mode build iterations** are now included in build events, enabling external tooling to track rebuild cycles ([#14166](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14166), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **`Dyn.pp` now prints valid OCaml literals** for `Char` (quoted), `Int32`/`Int64`/`Nativeint` (with literal suffixes), and `Float` (including `infinity`, `neg_infinity`, `nan`) ([#13394](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13394), fixes [#13378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13378), by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- **Auto-injection of menhir lower bound** in generated opam files when the menhir extension is used, preventing build failures with older menhir installations ([#14168](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14168), fixes [#10707](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10707), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **Duplicate dune version bounds fixed** in generated opam files — no more `{>= "2.7" & >= "2.7"}` redundancy ([#14175](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14175), fixes [#3916](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3916), [#11106](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/11106), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **Inline tests now execute for byte-only libraries**: when a library has `(modes byte)`, the inline test runner is linked in byte mode so tests run correctly ([#14174](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14174), fixes [#9757](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9757), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **`dune init` now generates correct opam files** ([#14192](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14192), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
repositories.activity_summary: Active week with 228 files changed and 13 PRs still open, as the team prepares for the 3.23.0 release and continues major infrastructure work on RPC and the type system.
repositories.activity:
- [Per-module inter-library dependency filtering](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14116) remains under intensive review by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb), targeting [#4572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4572); a 40-70% null-build regression is being actively investigated
- [Port `Async_io` to `Lev` (libev)](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14243) opened by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) to replace the existing `select`-based I/O loop and lift the 1024 fd limit
- [Bring back action runners with bubblewrap sandbox](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14240) in draft by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg), intended for beta testing on the dev team ahead of 3.24
- [`%{pkg:foo:lib}` pforms for package install layouts](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14200) opened by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), allowing inspection of installed package contents and fixing [#3378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3378) and [#14193](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14193)
- [Add `(archived false)` field to library stanza](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14144) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), introducing unarchived libraries that link object files individually
- [Add reference docs for `dune tools`](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14198) by [Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247) in response to [#14184](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14184)
- [Replace revdeps-dev-build workflow with a full Nix derivation](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14228) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) to fix OxCaml trunk CI ([#14214](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14214))
- [Add OxCaml CI Nix job](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14248) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) to check OxCaml compatibility in CI
- [Melange: use `melc --modules` instead of `ocamldep`](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14195) merged by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- [3.22.2 release](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14112) was completed and merged during this week, with the 3.23.0 release tracker ([#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) opened targeting 2026-04-20
- A new `Fd.t` managed file descriptor type introduced in `Stdune` to replace bare `Unix.file_descr` throughout the codebase, improving safety ([#14202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14202), [#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14205), [#14206](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14206), [#14207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14207), [#14210](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14210), [#14216](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14216))
- Extensive `Repr.t` infrastructure expansion — adding constructors for `Int32`, `Int64`, `Nativeint`, `Bytes`, `Char`, `Float`, and `Quadruple`, plus a `make_compare` combinator ([#14236](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14236))
repositories.discussions_summary: Key ongoing discussions focus on the 3.23.0 release blockers, per-module dep filtering performance, a long-requested feature to declare depexts directly in dune-project, and the long-term plan for `dune monitor`.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **3.23.0 release tracker** ([#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), targeting 2026-04-20; current blockers include sandboxed package dep regression ([#14213](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14213)) and OxCaml CI compatibility ([#14214](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14214))
- **Sandboxed package deps are broken** ([#14213](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14213)) raised by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) as a regression for 3.23; a reproduction case is being developed in [#14189](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14189)
- **Allow arbitrary opam fields in `dune-project`** ([#14197](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14197)) opened by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV); [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed `(opam_fields (depexts "..."))` syntax as a lighter-weight alternative to opam.template files

- **Dune Monitor long-term plan** ([#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14121)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) outlines making `dune monitor` a standalone binary using Lwt instead of Fiber, with full Notty/Lwd rendering and RPC/trace-file observation
- **Per-module dep filtering performance** ([#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14116)): null-build regression of 40-70% under active investigation; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed memoizing library closure computation ([#14256](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14256)) as a mitigation step
- **Build fixes needed on illumos/Solaris** ([#14182](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14182)) reported by [Jonathan Perkin](https://github.com/jperkin), providing patches for `wait4()` semantics differences and `EINTR` handling in `read()`
repositories.trends_summary: The team is systematically improving infrastructure safety (managed Fd.t, Repr.t expansions), hardening the RPC layer, and preparing a new compile_commands.json feature for C/C++ editor tooling.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Systematic replacement of raw `Unix.file_descr` with a managed `Fd.t` type across the codebase, eliminating accidental double-close bugs and improving resource tracking
- Expansion of `Repr.t` runtime type representation, used to auto-derive comparisons and `to_dyn` functions, replacing handwritten boilerplate
- Growing investment in the Nix flake as the primary way to test OxCaml compatibility, with automatic weekly version-bump jobs and a dedicated derivation for OxCaml trunk builds
- RPC system being hardened and tested extensively ahead of upcoming changes: basic tests, lifecycle trace events, flaky shutdown fixes, and a planned migration to `libev` for higher fd limits
- Documentation improvements for package management: depexts explanation, package locking tutorial restructured with separate create/update/remove sections
repositories.issues_summary: Multiple long-standing issues closed this week including the `@all` target with `include_subdirs`, SIGKILL-without-SIGTERM, byte-only inline tests, duplicate opam version bounds, and `.h` file hygiene in `_build`.

repositories.brief_summary: Three PRs merged bringing OxCaml upstream support, polymorphic parameter fixes, and OCaml 5.5 compatibility to odoc.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml upstream support, OCaml 5.5 compat with module arrows and external types, polymorphic parameter parentheses fix.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml upstream support** merged into odoc, rebasing [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer)'s patches from the oxcaml/odoc fork via [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), allowing odoc to run on OxCaml codebases and adding OxCaml-specific syntax handling including modes in loader, layouts in loader, `iarray` syntax, labeled tuples, call position arguments, unboxed records, module type strengthening operator, and OxCaml tokens in the syntax highlighter ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** added by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd), including support for external types (rendered as abstract types) and module arrow types in signatures; path references to module arguments appear as unresolved links pending future refinement ([#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406))
- **Fixed missing parentheses** for polymorphic parameters in OxCaml rendering — `('a. 'a -> 'a list)` now renders with correct parentheses; polymorphic parameters are also expected in upstream OCaml 5.5 ([#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404))
repositories.activity_summary: OxCaml support effort formalized with a tracking issue and seven targeted sub-issues; three new OxCaml PRs opened covering modalities, kind annotations, and unboxed types.
repositories.activity:
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened tracking issue [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) to coordinate first-class OxCaml support in odoc, referencing seven specific feature sub-issues: **OxCaml polymorphic parameters** ([#1412](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1412), closed this week via [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404)), **unboxed types** ([#1413](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1413), replaced by [#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)), **layouts** ([#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415)), **modalities** ([#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416)), **modes** ([#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417)), **`@zero-alloc` annotation** ([#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418)), and **`include functor`** ([#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419))
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened three new OxCaml feature PRs, currently open and awaiting merge: **support for modalities** ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420), addresses [#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416)), **kind annotations** ([#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410), addresses [#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415)), and **unboxed named types** ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407), addresses [#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)); all three are currently in a conflicting state and need rebasing
- PR [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) adding **`@inline` tag support for module declarations**, generated by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) via Claude Code, was closed without merge by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) because cross-references inside inline modules point to the ungenerated subpage — resolving this requires new constructors in paths, references, and identifiers (unlike inlined includes, which always remain on the same HTML page)
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on module argument linking strategy in OCaml 5.5 PRs; detailed review feedback on @inline tag implementation identifying cross-reference resolution gaps.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In PR [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406), [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) discussed whether **module argument path components** like `M.t` in `(module M : T) -> M.t` should link to the `t` component of the module type; they concluded that improvements to this require more involved changes and deferred to a follow-up
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) provided detailed feedback on [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) clarifying that **`@inline` on module declarations differs fundamentally from inlined includes**: inlined includes stay on the same HTML page so links are unaffected, while inline modules would require tracking which modules will be inlined during path/reference resolution — analogous to the existing `OpaqueModule` constructor mechanism
repositories.trends_summary: Systematic OxCaml feature coverage effort underway with a tracking issue and dedicated PRs for each language feature gap.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- A **coordinated OxCaml support campaign** is taking shape: upstream compatibility ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)) landed first to unblock running odoc on OxCaml codebases, followed by individual PRs targeting each rendering gap (modes, layouts, modalities, unboxed types, kind annotations), organized under a single tracking issue ([#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411))
- **OCaml 5.5 feature parity** is progressing in parallel with OxCaml support, with module arrows ([#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)) and polymorphic parameters ([#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404)) both landing this week as features that are shared between OxCaml and upstream OCaml 5.5
repositories.contributors_summary: art-w drove OxCaml feature work with three PRs merged/opened; panglesd landed 5.5 compat; shonfeder created the OxCaml tracking infrastructure; jonludlam merged and reviewed.
repositories.contributors:
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) authored the OxCaml upstream PR ([#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)) and the polymorphic parameters fix ([#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404)), both merged this week, and opened three follow-on OxCaml feature PRs ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407), [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410), [#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)); also cherry-picked a fix into [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) authored and landed OCaml 5.5 compatibility ([#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)), rebased it after [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404) merged, and engaged in the module argument linking discussion
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) merged PRs [#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399), [#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404), and [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406), fixed test failures in [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) before merging, and provided detailed architectural review feedback on [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) explaining the cross-reference resolution challenges
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened the OxCaml tracking issue [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) and seven sub-issues ([#1412](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1412)–[#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419)) documenting each rendering gap with concrete OCaml examples
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) submitted [#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408) adding `@inline` tag support for module declarations, generated with Claude Code as an exploration of the work needed for [#564](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/564)
- [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) reviewed [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) and discussed module argument linking strategy

repositories.brief_summary: A busy week with syntax highlighting fixes, platform release scraper improvements, new backstage posts, and several platform tool changelogs.
repositories.new_features_summary: The platform release scraper gained auto-ignore and pre-release detection, and the Hilite MDX syntax highlighting was rolled out across all content parsers.
repositories.new_features:
- **Hilite MDX syntax highlighting** is now used consistently across backstage, changelog, cookbook, exercise, is-ocaml-yet, and platform release parsers — all switched from `Hilite.Md.transform` to `Markdown.Content.render ~syntax_highlighting:true` with MDX support enabled via [#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593). This fixes long-standing incorrect highlighting of OCaml interactive session syntax (the `#` prompt style).
- **Platform release scraper** now auto-detects pre-releases and ignores configured tag patterns per project. Tags matching `alpha`, `beta`, `rc[0-9]`, or `preview` are automatically marked `experimental: true`; dune is configured with `ignore_patterns = ["alpha"]` so dune alpha releases no longer appear on the site. A new `all_including_ignored()` function prevents the scraper from re-creating files it already knows about, via [#3616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3616).
- **Platform release parser** gains an `ignore` field on the `t` type, and `all()` now filters out entries where `ignore: true` is set in the data file, via [#3616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3616).
- **Install page fix**: dune was listed in descriptive text but missing from the actual `opam install` command — now corrected in both the install page and tutorial via [#3609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3609).

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with opam 2.5.1 landing, broad Windows/MSYS2 improvements, and a variety of new packages and library updates across the ecosystem.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages include OCaml GTK 4 bindings (ocgtk), a PPX for module type mixins, OCaml Lua bindings, an OpenRouter API client, and Azure Cosmos DB bindings.
repositories.new_features:
- **ocgtk 0.1~preview1** adds OCaml bindings for GTK 4 along with a new `conf-gtk4` virtual package providing depexts for major Linux distributions and macOS; the PR [#29729](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29729) is under active review by [Jan Midtgaard](https://github.com/jmid) with Windows support deferred to a follow-up.
- **ppx_mixins 0.1 and 0.2.0** ([#29741](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29741), [#29746](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29746)) introduce a new PPX that mixes module type signatures into type declarations, including module type extensions allowing `[%mixins Printable + Comparable]` syntax; submitted by [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho) (Sacha Ayoun).
- **ezlua 0.1** ([#29655](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29655)) adds OCaml-Lua codec runtime and high-level interpreter API by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube) (Simon Cruanes).
- **ocaml-ai-sdk 0.2** ([#29724](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29724)) adds smooth stream transformers, retry with exponential backoff, OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry, and Unicode-aware chunking modes to the AI provider abstraction SDK by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy) (José Nogueira).
- **openrouter_api 0.0.1** ([#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) is a new Async-based OCaml client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API, covering chat completions with streaming, tools, and structured outputs, submitted by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret).
- **azure-cosmos-db 0.4.0** (3 packages, [#29682](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29682)) adds Eio and Lwt backends for the Azure Cosmos DB interface by [Morten Knaack](https://github.com/mknaack) (Morten Knaack).
- **nats-client 0.0.7** ([#29720](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29720)) releases an updated lean OCaml NATS protocol client with improved opam-ci compatibility, by [Heb](https://github.com/Hebilicious) (Heb).
- **atd 4.1.0** (13 packages, [#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709)) adds new `atd-jsonlike` and `atd-yamlx` packages, `<json repr="object">` for externally-tagged sum types, and OCaml >= 4.14 as minimum, by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) (Martin Jambon).
- **jasmin 2026.03.1** ([#29723](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29723)) adds the Jasmin compiler release by [Jean-Christophe Léchenet](https://github.com/eponier) (Jean-Christophe Léchenet).
repositories.activity_summary: opam 2.5.1 was released and merged along with alert upgrades, dune 3.22.2 landed, and a batch of library releases including ffmpeg, liquidsoap, alt-ergo, and postgresql arrived.

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml support merged (140 files, Float32/int8/int16/null types) alongside JSPI documentation clarifications.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml support adds Float32, small int types, null types, unboxed indexing, and new atomic/domain primitives.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml (Jane Street's OCaml fork) support** merged into master via [#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105), adding cross-compiler compatibility across 140 files. This includes new runtime files `runtime/js/float32.js` and `runtime/wasm/float32.wat` implementing **Float32 (32-bit float)** operations, `runtime/js/domain.js` and `runtime/wasm/domain.wat` additions for **domain TLS** (`caml_domain_tls_get`/`set`) and new **atomic primitives** (`caml_atomic_compare_exchange`, `caml_atomic_add`, `caml_atomic_sub`, `caml_atomic_land`, etc.).
- **Small integer types** (`int8_u`, `int16_u`) supported in both the JS and Wasm backends ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), with new test suites under `compiler/tests-oxcaml/small-ints/`.
- **Unboxed int indexing** for strings and arrays, including `compiler/tests-oxcaml/unboxed_int/` with extensive string/array indexing tests ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)).
- **Float16 <-> float conversion primitives** and unboxed-to-boxed **immutable array conversion** primitives exposed for OxCaml compatibility ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)).
- **Async exception support** via `Sys.with_async_exns` and **recursive values with mixed blocks** implemented for OxCaml ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)).
- **CI workflows for OxCaml** added under `.github/workflows/`, with a new `tools/ci_setup-oxcaml.ml` setup tool and primitive coverage checks ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)).
- **JSPI effects availability** clarified in documentation: `--effects=jspi` (the default for wasm_of_ocaml) now works out-of-the-box in Chrome 137 and Node.js 25+, with `--effects=cps` recommended for other browsers ([#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213)). Updates cover `README_wasm_of_ocaml.md`, `manual/options.wiki`, and `manual/wasm_overview.wiki`.
repositories.activity_summary: Two open Wasm optimization PRs and two blocked Wasm feature PRs remain active alongside a long-standing jsooTop WIP draft.
repositories.activity:
- **Wasm toplevel function size reduction** is being pursued via two open PRs by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon): [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) puts constant expressions at toplevel into global variables (halving the PRT benchmark toplevel function size), and draft [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) extracts loops from the toplevel function into helper functions so that Wasm engine tiering (Turbofan/V8 optimization) only applies to small helpers rather than the large, once-called init function.
- **WASI runtime** ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) and **Wasm native effects via Stack Switching** ([#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)) remain blocked on [#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177), [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613), and a new dune release. The WASI PR supports `--enable wasi` and `--enable exnref` flags for producing Wasm binaries runnable with Wizard or wasmtime.
- **Alternative jsooTop interface** ([#833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/833)) remains open as a long-standing WIP draft, providing `JsooTopWrapped` with first-class error/warning values and an Lwt-based `JsooTopAsynchronous` implementation.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on JSPI documentation and Wasm optimization code clarity arose in open PRs.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In [#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189), [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) and [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) discussed whether `--experimental-wasm-imported-strings` belongs in user documentation given that a polyfill fallback exists, prompting the separate [#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213) clarification PR that was subsequently merged.
- In [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) requested an explanatory comment for a specific constant value used in the global variable optimization, flagging a readability concern before merge.
repositories.trends_summary: OxCaml compatibility and Wasm backend optimization (code size and engine tiering) are the dominant development threads.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml ecosystem compatibility** is becoming a first-class concern: the merged [#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105) adds dedicated CI pipelines, runtime files, and test suites mirroring the mainstream OCaml support structure, signaling ongoing investment in supporting Jane Street's extended OCaml variant.
- **Wasm backend code-size and startup optimization** is an active focus, with both [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) targeting the toplevel/init function specifically — a recurring bottleneck for Wasm engines.
- **JSPI standardization progress** (Chrome 137, Node.js 25 as stable defaults) is shifting documentation from experimental caveats toward clear browser version guidance, reflected in [#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213).
repositories.contributors_summary: Jérôme Vouillon authored all merged and open PRs; @hhugo provided code review feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) authored and merged the **OxCaml support** PR ([#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) and the **JSPI documentation clarification** PR ([#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213)), and opened the active optimization PRs ([#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211), [#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)). Commits authored this week include workarounds for `caml_sys_exit` arity mismatches in OxCaml native dynlink and fixes to toplevel bytecode reification.
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reviewed [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and [#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105), requesting code clarity improvements, and contributed to the JSPI documentation discussion in [#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189).

repositories.brief_summary: Major week: functor inlining limits, non_pointer caml_modify skipping, new name mangling, static evaluator, and scannable axes separability.
repositories.new_features_summary: Functor inlining size limits, -Ix flag, non_pointer caml_modify skip, reversible name mangling, metaprogramming promoted to beta, static evaluator.
repositories.new_features:
- **Separate functor inlining size limits** added by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell), introducing `-flambda2-small-functor-size` and `-flambda2-large-functor-size` flags with a default large threshold set at twice that for functions; large functors are now ineligible for speculative inlining unless annotated, and `inline available` attributes were added to Stdlib's `Hashtbl`, `Map`, and `Set` functors ([#5878](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5878))
- New **`-Ix <dir>` compiler flag** added by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) to mark include directories where CMX files are guaranteed to always be available, as infrastructure for future layout polymorphism error reporting ([#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654))
- **`caml_modify` is now skipped** for `non_pointer` types by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), improving codegen for types whose values are never GC-traced pointers; also bumps the mixed block version to v6 ([#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831))
- **New reversible name mangling scheme** (`Structured_mangling` module) introduced by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), and Samuel Hym (Tarides) — encodes arbitrary strings into output-safe identifiers with full reversibility, used for parameterized library symbols ([#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099))
- **Runtime metaprogramming promoted from alpha to beta extension** by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr), reflecting increased maturity of the feature ([#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843))
- **First version of static evaluator** for layout polymorphism merged by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) — a lambda calculus evaluator for statically evaluating layout-polymorphic expressions; inter-module evaluation not yet supported ([#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512))
- **Scannable axes: separability becomes a scannable axis** in a large type system refactoring by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) — merges `Pointerness` into an expanded `Separability` lattice (`non_pointer < non_pointer64 < non_float < separable < maybe_separable`), renames the base sort `Value` to `Scannable` internally, and redefines `immediate` as `scannable non_pointer` ([#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))
- **Patricia tree bit-packed integer representation** merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) (credits to Jules Jacob for the original bit-packing representation), improving internal data structure efficiency in Flambda2 ([#5833](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5833))
- **Additional `union_total` function families** for patricia trees added by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs), avoiding allocations in common union operations ([#5803](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5803))
- **Tick thread state reset after `fork()`** in child process by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark), reinitializing mutex and restarting the tick thread in child processes to maintain support for `Unix.fork()` in multithreaded programs ([#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829))
- **Graceful error reporting** when runtime metaprogramming encounters unsupported language features, by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian), replacing internal errors with user-facing messages ([#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823)); also fixed a REPL issue with the metaprogramming syntax directive ([#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801))
- **Profiling counter for CMX loading** added by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) to the Flambda2 pipeline for tracking compilation performance ([#5861](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5861))
- **Expanded documentation** for value layouts, nullability, and separability in the unboxed types extension guide (`jane/doc/extensions/_03-unboxed-types/01-intro.md`), with detailed descriptions of the nullability and separability axes and their relationship to `immediate` ([#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs opened include GADT match fix, scannable axes on abstract kinds, alloc mode refactoring, and multiple 5.4 merge fixes; Reaper refactoring continues.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened PRs** this week include [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)'s fix for lambda_to_flambda on GADT matches ([#5879](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5879)), [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)'s support for scannable axes on abstract kinds ([#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874)), [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges)'s alloc mode for returns refactoring ([#5877](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5877), draft) and diamond-aware mode error hints ([#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858)), and [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr)'s draft layout polymorphic functions PR ([#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870))
- **5.4 merge work** expanded significantly with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) opening fixes for effect syntax compilation ([#5880](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5880)), two more test failures ([#5881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5881)), a runtime objects test fix ([#5883](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5883)), an Iarray re-oxidise PR ([#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882)), and a jkind error printer fix ([#5867](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5867)); [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened the merge of remaining minus-31 changes ([#5853](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5853))
- **Reaper (Flambda2 dead code elimination)** saw three merged cleanup/refactoring PRs: splitting `dep_solver.ml` ([#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)), cleanup after the split ([#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)), and addressing Basile's review comments ([#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs); code size computation when rebuilding ([#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) remains open
- **Register allocator improvements**: [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) opened two PRs for affinity-aware IRC coalescing ([#5863](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5863)) and minor refactoring of affinity data structures ([#5849](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5849)), and merged a fix to the split-pass occur check ([#5537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5537))
- **Layout polymorphism infrastructure**: Multiple foundational PRs merged — `update_level` fix ([#5791](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5791)), layout var `fatal_error` robustness ([#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817)), `val_lpoly`/`val_type` serialization in one go ([#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835)), and `lpoly generalize` simplification ([#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792)) all by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn); [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) opened a draft for weak parameterized symbols ([#5873](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5873)) as an experimental deduplication approach
- **5.4 runtime merge** ([#5598](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5598)) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) continues with pending PRs including free list sync on orphaned pools ([#5845](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5845)), `caml_raise_async` fix ([#5846](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5846)), and Unix labels test fix ([#5847](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5847))
- **Flambda2 simplifier precision** improvements: [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) opened improved alloc_mode use when building unknown types ([#5820](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5820)), [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) opened continuation argument type refinement based on arity ([#5850](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5850)), and [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened improved `argument_types_useful` check to reduce unnecessary speculative inlining ([#5760](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5760))
- **Merlin integration**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened an update to the Merlin import script to pull from the local workspace ([#5872](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5872), draft) and the larger PR adding Merlin as a subtree ([#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)) remains open
- **Flambda2 CMX optimization** PR from [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) to move export info out of the Marshal block into a separate section for faster linking ([#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809)) remains open with discussion
- **Testsuite**: [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) merged the large PR moving mlexamples tests into the main testsuite ([#5346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5346), 12k additions/42k deletions) and has a PR open to add fexpr output checking to toplevel expect tests ([#5837](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5837))
- **Various bug fixes merged**: fix for naked int8/int16 kind errors in `Type_grammar.must_be_singleton` ([#5852](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5852)), consistent nullability computation in typeopt ([#5841](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5841)), staging handling in `Printtyp` ([#5785](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5785)), macOS compilation fix ([#5714](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5714))
- **Initial preemption implementation** ([#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4881)) by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) remains open, adding runtime effects-based preemption for concurrency schedulers
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussions on kind system structure for scannable axes on abstract kinds, mode error hint precision for diamond lattices, and .cmx export info optimization.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- The **scannable axes on abstract kinds** PR ([#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874)) prompted a design discussion between [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) about where to place `Scannable_axes.t` in the kind representation — specifically whether it should live on `base_and_axes` or within each kind constructor, with consensus that the current approach is a reasonable interim pending a fuller kind system redesign
- **Diamond-aware hints** ([#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858)) generated discussion between [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) about how to correctly select which branch of a Meet/Join hint is "at fault" in diamond-shaped mode lattices, settling on using left-adjoint preservation of colimits to pick the branch rather than maintaining tight mode-level chains
- **Alloc mode for returns** ([#5877](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5877), draft) received extensive early review from [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) identifying numerous places where `alloc_mode` should be changed to `return_mode`, particularly around `Lsend`, `Fexpr.alloc_mode_for_applications`, and `Cmx_format.alloc_mode`
- **Flambda2 CMX export info** PR ([#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809)) by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) proposed moving export data out of the inline Marshal block into a `.cmx` section to speed up linking, with [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) looping in [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) for awareness
- The **external contributor's portability fix** ([#5848](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5848) by [AmberDevs](https://github.com/amber181)) was closed and replaced with an identical internal PR ([#5865](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5865)) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) due to administrative overhead for external contributions, prompting a sad response from the contributor
repositories.trends_summary: Flambda2 Reaper refactoring, 5.4 merge acceleration, layout poly infrastructure buildout, and runtime metaprogramming maturation are the dominant themes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **Flambda2 Reaper** (dead code elimination) component is receiving intensive refactoring: `dep_solver.ml` was split into separate points-to analysis, unboxing analysis, and types rewrite modules ([#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776), [#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787), [#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)), with code size computation ([#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) still pending — suggesting an upcoming phase of new Reaper capabilities
- **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** is in an active test-fixing phase, with many small PRs resolving individual test suite failures across the typing, runtime, and effects subsystems; the merge branch has consumed significant bandwidth this week
- **Layout polymorphism** infrastructure is expanding in multiple directions simultaneously: static evaluation ([#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512)), persistent module staticity ([#5834](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5834)), CMI serialization improvements ([#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835)), and `env_alloc_mode` for `Tpat_fun_layout` ([#5662](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5662))
- **Runtime metaprogramming** is progressing through a staged rollout: promoted from alpha to beta this week, with work on improving error reporting, REPL compatibility, and experimental deduplication via weak symbols ([#5873](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5873))
- **Patricia tree optimizations** continue to land incrementally: bit-packed integer representation ([#5833](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5833)), allocation-avoiding `union_total` families ([#5803](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5803)), and a pending high-bits flag proposal ([#5543](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5543))
- **IRC register allocator** is seeing a series of refinements from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): affinity-aware coalescing ([#5863](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5863)), split-pass occur check ([#5537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5537)), and structural refactoring ([#5849](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5849))
