repositories.brief_summary: Signature-help improvements merged: optional argument highlighting and let..in binding fixes, with compiler sync and typeopt cleanup PRs opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: Signature-help now correctly highlights optional/labelled arguments and triggers on unfinished let..in bindings.
repositories.new_features:
- **Signature-help optional argument highlighting** fixed so that already-provided labelled arguments are skipped and optional arguments are correctly surfaced as the active parameter when initiated with `~` — previously the feature always highlighted the first argument regardless of what had been written ([#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216))
- **Signature-help trigger on unfinished `let...in` bindings** test case added reproducing the bug where signature-help failed to show anything when the `in` keyword was missing from a binding ([#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215))
repositories.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened covering compiler sync, typeopt cleanup, and syntax-doc improvements, alongside ongoing signature-help work.
repositories.activity:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened [#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234) to merge compiler changes from `5.2.0minus-37`, a large diff (202 files) that notably handles parsing of both `-I` and `-Ix` flags from [oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654)
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened [#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233) to reduce the diff between the compiler and Merlin in `typeopt.ml`/`typeopt.mli` by correctly commenting out irrelevant sections and improving error handling
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened [#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235) to improve syntax-doc support for scannable axes in Merlin's syntax-doc feature
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)'s [#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223) fixing `let..in` binding signature-help is now conflicting after [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215) and [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) were merged, and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) is waiting for a rebase before reviewing
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo)'s [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) adding tests for signature-help with only labelled arguments has a failing CI that [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) flagged this week
repositories.discussions_summary: Review coordination on signature-help PRs: rebase needed on #223 after #215 and #216 merged, and CI failure flagged on #205.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) noted in [#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223) that the PR contains the diffs of the now-merged [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215) and [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216), and is holding off review until [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) rebases or merges with main
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) flagged in [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) that CI is now genuinely failing for the labelled-argument signature-help test
repositories.trends_summary: Signature-help reliability is an active focus, with multiple PRs improving argument tracking and triggering behaviour.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Signature-help correctness** is receiving sustained attention from [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo), with a series of PRs ([#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205), [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215), [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216), [#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223)) targeting edge cases in labelled, optional, and `let..in` binding contexts, downstreaming fixes from upstream [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)
- **Compiler diff reduction** is an ongoing maintenance theme, with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) actively syncing upstream compiler changes ([#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234)) and reducing divergence in individual files like `typeopt` ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233))
repositories.contributors_summary: Charlène_Gros authored both merged PRs improving signature-help; Liam Stevenson opened three new PRs and provided review coordination.
repositories.contributors:
- [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) authored the two merged PRs this week: [#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) fixing optional argument highlighting in signature-help and [#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215) adding a cram test reproducing the `let..in` binding trigger bug; also has open PRs [#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223) and [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) in progress
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened three PRs ([#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233), [#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234), [#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235)) and provided review coordination on [#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223) and [#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)

repositories.brief_summary: CI fixes for ocaml-lsp-server, odoc, sherlodoc, and macOS dependencies opened by Matthew Else.
repositories.activity_summary: Two new PRs address CI failures; a long-running draft PR on package patching infrastructure saw discussion.
repositories.activity:
- [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) **Fix the CI**, cherry-picking fixes from the dev branch to repair builds of **odoc**, **sherlodoc**, and a macOS-breaking sherlodoc upstream dependency; the PR also pins `lwt` and `utop` to the versions in this repo, pins `checkseum` to the tip of its repo (tracking upstream fix [ocaml/opam-repository#29747](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29747)), and backports a `cut`-fix patch for macOS
- [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened and then closed [#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) **fix ocaml-lsp-server** (incorporating [oxcaml/ocaml-lsp#10](https://github.com/oxcaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/10)), superseded by the broader [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s draft PR [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) **Allow multiple versions of upstream patched packages** received new discussion on 2026-04-14; the PR proposes replacing the large `conflicts` clause in `ocaml-variants.5.2.0+ox` with an auto-generated `oxcaml-patch-guards` package and per-package `oxcaml-<pkg>` / `oxcaml-<pkg>-patches` guards produced by `layout.sh`
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on PR #27 explored whether layout.sh belongs in this repo or in internal release infrastructure.
repositories.notable_discussions: - In [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27), [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) asked whether `layout.sh` should live in internal release infra rather than this public repo; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) noted that keeping it here makes it easier for external contributors to add patched package versions and that the unified branch already uses it to keep guards up to date
repositories.trends_summary: CI reliability and the patching infrastructure for upstream OxCaml-patched packages are the active focus areas.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **CI stability** is an active concern: both [#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) and [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) address build failures across odoc, sherlodoc, ocaml-lsp-server, and macOS-specific issues, suggesting the CI surface area for this overlay repository is still being stabilised
- **Package patching governance** is under design: [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) represents an ongoing effort to move from hand-edited `conflicts` lists to a generated, scriptable approach for managing which patched upstream packages are permitted in OxCaml switches
repositories.contributors_summary: Matthew Else drove CI fixes this week; David Allsopp's patching infrastructure draft continued to receive discussion.
repositories.contributors:
- [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened two PRs targeting CI failures: [#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) (closed as superseded) and [#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) (open, fixes odoc, sherlodoc, macOS, lwt, utop, checkseum)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) is the author of the long-running draft [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) and engaged in discussion about the `layout.sh` placement question raised by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js)
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) contributed a design question in [#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27) about the appropriate home for the patch-guard generation script

repositories.brief_summary: Windows backend CPU bug reported with a root cause identified in poll set management; unlink PR awaits review.
repositories.activity_summary: An open PR for `Eio.Path.unlink` saw follow-up; a new Windows backend high-CPU issue was filed with a proposed fix.
repositories.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) followed up on the open **`missing_ok` parameter for `Eio.Path.unlink`** PR ([#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)), asking [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) for guidance on what changes are needed for acceptance; this PR addresses the underlying feature request tracked in [#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)
- [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) filed a new issue ([#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837)) reporting **100% CPU usage on Windows** with the `eio_windows` backend when using `Fiber.both` and `Eio.Flow.copy` for a bidirectional TCP proxy workload — the same pattern works without issue on Linux
repositories.discussions_summary: Root cause for the Windows high-CPU bug was identified in the `sched.ml` poll set update logic.
repositories.notable_discussions: - In [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837), [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) identified the root cause in `lib/eio_windows/sched.ml`: the `update` function fails to remove a file descriptor from `to_write` when transitioning to read-only mode (and vice versa for write-only), causing both read and write events to be polled simultaneously and spinning the scheduler — a locally patched version confirmed the fix resolves the high CPU usage
repositories.trends_summary: Windows backend reliability is surfacing as an area needing attention, with a concrete poll-management bug now identified.
repositories.emerging_trends: - **Windows backend correctness** is under scrutiny: [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) exposes a latent poll-set bookkeeping bug in `eio_windows` that causes busy-looping under real-world bidirectional I/O workloads, suggesting that Windows-specific code paths may benefit from more systematic review and testing
repositories.contributors_summary: Zhang Rui filed a Windows bug with a detailed MWE and root cause; Volker Diels-Grabsch followed up on an open unlink PR.
repositories.contributors:
- [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) opened issue [#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) with a minimal working example reproducing the Windows high-CPU bug, and followed up in the same issue with a code-level root cause analysis pointing to the missing poll-set cleanup in `eio_windows/sched.ml`
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) commented on PR [#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833) seeking review feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) to move the `missing_ok` unlink feature forward

group_summaries.brief_summary: opam 2.5.1 released; Windows/MSYS2 improvements, new OCaml packages (GTK4, AI tooling), and syntax highlighting fixes on ocaml.org.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages include GTK4 bindings, Lua bindings, AI SDK 0.2, Azure Cosmos DB backends; ocaml.org gains MDX-aware syntax highlighting.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **ocgtk 0.1~preview1** adds OCaml bindings for GTK 4 along with a new `conf-gtk4` virtual package providing depexts for major Linux distributions and macOS via [ocaml/opam-repository#29729](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29729); Windows support is deferred to a follow-up.
- **ppx_mixins 0.1 and 0.2.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29741](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29741), [ocaml/opam-repository#29746](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29746)) introduce a PPX for mixing module type signatures into type declarations, including `[%mixins Printable + Comparable]` syntax; submitted by [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho).
- **ezlua 0.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29655](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29655)) adds an OCaml-Lua codec runtime and high-level interpreter API by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube).
- **ocaml-ai-sdk 0.2** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29724](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29724)) adds stream transformers, retry with exponential backoff, OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry, and Unicode-aware chunking modes, by [José Nogueira](https://github.com/thatportugueseguy).
- **openrouter_api 0.0.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) is a new Async-based OCaml client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API covering chat completions with streaming, tools, and structured outputs, submitted by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret).
- **azure-cosmos-db 0.4.0** (3 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29682](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29682)) adds Eio and Lwt backends for the Azure Cosmos DB interface by [Morten Knaack](https://github.com/mknaack).
- **atd 4.1.0** (13 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709)) adds `atd-jsonlike` and `atd-yamlx` packages plus `<json repr="object">` for externally-tagged sum types, by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon).
- **Hilite MDX syntax highlighting** is now used consistently across all ocaml.org content parsers (backstage, changelog, cookbook, exercise, is-ocaml-yet, platform releases) via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593), fixing long-standing incorrect highlighting of OCaml interactive session syntax.
- **Platform release scraper** now auto-detects pre-releases and supports per-project ignore patterns via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3616); tags matching `alpha`, `beta`, `rc[0-9]`, or `preview` are marked `experimental: true` automatically.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) had a high-activity week: **opam 2.5.1** landed ([ocaml/opam-repository#29731](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29731)), **dune 3.22.2** fixed a `--diff-command` regression ([ocaml/opam-repository#29704](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29704)), and numerous new packages and updates were merged across domains including multimedia, databases, formal verification, and AI tooling.
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) focused on content quality and tooling: syntax highlighting was rolled out across all parsers ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593)), the platform release scraper gained pre-release detection ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3616)), and new backstage posts covered wasm_of_ocaml and Merlin.
- Cross-repository coordination was evident: **opam 2.5.1** and **dune 3.22.2** releases were published to opam-repository and simultaneously documented on ocaml.org by the same contributors.
- Windows/MSYS2 ecosystem support saw a concentrated batch of improvements including UCRT64 conf package updates and several compatibility fixes ([ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579)).
- The OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness tracker ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)) continues to be monitored, with platform tools (opam, dune, merlin, lsp-server, odoc, ocamlformat) and key ecosystem libraries tracked.
group_summaries.activity_summary: opam 2.5.1 and dune 3.22.2 released; ffmpeg, liquidsoap, alt-ergo, postgresql, and sqlite3 updated; ocaml.org content and scraper tooling improved.
group_summaries.activity:
- **opam 2.5.1** (8 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29731](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29731)) released by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), followed by an upgrade alert update noting NixOS stable 25.11 remains on 2.4.x ([ocaml/opam-repository#29733](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29733)); documented on ocaml.org in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3614).
- **dune 3.22.2** (18 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29704](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29704)) fixes a `--diff-command` regression from 3.22.1 by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder); changelog added to ocaml.org in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3608).
- **ffmpeg 1.3.0** (8 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29703](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29703)) and **liquidsoap 2.4.3** (4 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29708](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29708), [ocaml/opam-repository#29715](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29715)) merged by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots).
- **alt-ergo 2.6.3** (4 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29722](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29722)) adds `ae.pow_int` and `ae.pow_real` support by [Pierre Villemot](https://github.com/Halbaroth).
- **postgresql 5.4.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29738](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29738)) exposes the libpq socket as `Unix.file_descr` and **sqlite3 5.4.1** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29737](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29737)) releases the OCaml runtime lock around more SQLite API calls, both by [Markus Mottl](https://github.com/mmottl).
- **elpi 3.7.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29728](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29728)) adds dependency computation API, separate scoping AST, and REPL improvements by [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares).
- **Caqti 2.3.0** (7 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29690](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29690)) maintenance release with bugfixes by [Petter A. Urkedal](https://github.com/paurkedal).
- **albatross 2.7.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29712](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29712)) adds BHyve virtual machine support for MirageOS unikernel orchestration by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm).
- **ochre and ochre-cli 1.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29700](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29700)), **textmate-language 0.6.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29706](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29706)), and **tm-grammars 2.0.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29717](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29717)) form a syntax-highlighting toolchain update by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) and [Alan](https://github.com/alan-j-hu).
- **html_of_jsx 0.0.9** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29716](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29716)) adds JSX pretty-printing and render performance improvements by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx).
- **runtime_events_tools 0.5.4** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29694](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29694)) reinstates `olly latency` and fixes cmdliner 2.0 compatibility by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist).
- **ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 and merlin 5.7.0-504** changelogs added to ocaml.org by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3605).
- **Backstage posts** published on wasm_of_ocaml ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3612](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3612)) and Merlin signature help ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3610)) by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine).
- **discuss.ocaml.org announcements** for platform releases added via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3619](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3619).
- **Why3 1.8.2** compatibility extended to OCaml 5.5 and Rocq 9 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29734](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29734)) by [Guillaume Melquiond](https://github.com/silene).
- **checkseum 0.5.3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29747](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29747)) fixes clang 16.0.6 compilation by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure).
- **qcow 0.14.0** (4 packages, [ocaml/opam-repository#29696](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29696)) fixes handling of images larger than 4TB by [Pau Ruiz Safont](https://github.com/psafont).
- **vsrocq-language-server 2.4.1 and 2.4.3** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29727](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29727), [ocaml/opam-repository#29732](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29732)) released via `vscoqbot` automation.
- **MDX CI test setup** opened by [Yassine Haouzane](https://github.com/YassineHaouzane) in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3622).
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **opam 2.5.1** was released to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) via [ocaml/opam-repository#29731](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29731) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), who also added the corresponding platform release entry on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3614).
- **dune 3.22.2** was submitted to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) via [ocaml/opam-repository#29704](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29704) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), who also added the changelog to [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3608) (having also filed the dune 3.22.1 changelog in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3596](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3596)).
- The **OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness** meta-issue ([ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463)) tracks opam, dune, merlin, lsp-server, odoc, and ocamlformat, all of which have corresponding release entries and changelogs on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org).
- **Syntax highlighting tooling** (ochre/textmate-language/tm-grammars) landed in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) while [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) simultaneously rolled out improved syntax highlighting for OCaml code examples site-wide via [ocaml/ocaml.org#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593), reflecting parallel investment in OCaml code presentation across the ecosystem.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- The **MSYS2 UCRT64 transition** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579), closing [ocaml/opam-repository#29575](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29575), [ocaml/opam-repository#29576](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29576), [ocaml/opam-repository#29577](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29577), [ocaml/opam-repository#29578](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29578)) was merged by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto): six `conf-mingw-w64-*-x86_64` packages now support UCRT64 alongside MINGW64, prompted by MSYS2's deprecation of the MINGW64 environment in March 2026.
- **OCaml 5.5.0 release readiness** is tracked in [ocaml/opam-repository#29463](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29463) by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), covering platform tools and key ecosystem libraries.
- **conf-brotli** required two rounds of fixes ([ocaml/opam-repository#29718](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29718), [ocaml/opam-repository#29726](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29726)) to address broken build field syntax and Cygwin-specific issues, involving discussion of correct opam filter syntax for pkgconf arguments on Windows.
- **stdcompat incompatibility** with OCaml 4.14.3 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29667](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29667)) and upper bounds for js_of_ocaml-compiler ([ocaml/opam-repository#29714](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29714)) were addressed to prevent widespread build failures.
- **wadi 0.1.0** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29620](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29620)), a Dune-free OCaml build system using a single `wadi.toml` manifest with content-based incremental builds, is awaiting further action from submitter [@improvingjef](https://github.com/improvingjef).
- **combobulate Emacs integration** note ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603)) opened by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) prompted a request from [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) to also replace `ocaml-eglot` with `ocaml-eglot-mode` in the same file.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Windows/MSYS2 improvements** are accumulating: UCRT64 support for mingw-w64 conf packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579)), MSYS2 depexts for conf-m4/conf-perl/conf-findutils, and multiple conf-brotli ([ocaml/opam-repository#29718](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29718), [ocaml/opam-repository#29726](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29726)) and camlidl ([ocaml/opam-repository#29653](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29653)) fixes reflect sustained investment in Windows OCaml support.
- **AI/LLM tooling** is growing in the ecosystem: `ocaml-ai-sdk` 0.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29724](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29724)) adds streaming and telemetry infrastructure, and `openrouter_api` 0.0.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) provides access to multiple LLM providers.
- **wasm_of_ocaml** is receiving documentation attention: the backstage post ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3612](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3612)) covers 6.1–6.3 changes and highlights dynlink/toplevel support as a path to running the OCaml Playground on wasm_of_ocaml.
- **Editor tooling documentation** is a recurring focus: the combobulate Emacs PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3603)) and the Merlin signature help backstage post ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3610)) both highlight continued investment in the OCaml editor experience.
- **Syntax highlighting correctness** for OCaml interactive sessions was addressed at both the library level in opam-repository (textmate-language 0.6.0 via [ocaml/opam-repository#29706](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29706)) and the site level on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593)).
- **x-maintenance-intent** annotations are being encouraged more consistently as part of the archiving policy workflow in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository).

group_summaries.brief_summary: opam 2.5.1 security release, dune approaching 3.23.0, and OxCaml support merged into odoc and js_of_ocaml.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune gains compile_commands.json, SIGTERM grace period, and opam workflow changes; odoc and js_of_ocaml add OxCaml support; opam 2.5.1 fixes path-escape vulnerability.
group_summaries.new_features:
**[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**
- **compile_commands.json generation** for C/C++ foreign stubs produced as part of `@check` when using `(lang dune 3.23)`, enabling clangd and other tools ([ocaml/dune#14185](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14185), by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- **SIGTERM before SIGKILL** sent to child processes on shutdown and watch-mode cancellation, closing a 7-year-old issue ([ocaml/dune#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14224), fixes [ocaml/dune#2445](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/2445), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **Opam file generation now requires explicit promotion** via `dune promote`; generation triggered by `@install`, `@runtest`, and `@opam`; in release mode source opam files are used directly ([ocaml/dune#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg)) — requires dune 3.23
- **RPC lifecycle trace events** added for accepting clients and shutting down the RPC server ([ocaml/dune#14232](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14232), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Watch mode build iterations** included in build events, enabling external tooling to track rebuild cycles ([ocaml/dune#14166](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14166), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Auto-injection of menhir lower bound** in generated opam files when the menhir extension is used ([ocaml/dune#14168](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14168), fixes [ocaml/dune#10707](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10707), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **Duplicate dune version bounds fixed** in generated opam files ([ocaml/dune#14175](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14175), fixes [ocaml/dune#3916](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3916), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **Inline tests now execute for byte-only libraries** when a library has `(modes byte)` ([ocaml/dune#14174](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14174), fixes [ocaml/dune#9757](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9757), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- **`Dyn.pp` now prints valid OCaml literals** for `Char`, `Int32`/`Int64`/`Nativeint`, and `Float` ([ocaml/dune#13394](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13394), by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))

**[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**
- **OxCaml upstream support** merged, rebasing [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer)'s patches from the oxcaml/odoc fork via [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), adding OxCaml-specific syntax handling including modes, layouts, iarray, labeled tuples, and unboxed records ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399))
- **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** added by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd), including support for external types and module arrow types in signatures ([ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406))
- **Fixed missing parentheses** for polymorphic parameters in OxCaml rendering ([ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404), by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w))

**[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**
- **opam 2.5.1 released** ([ocaml/opam#6899](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6899)), a security and bug-fix release merged by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- **Security fix (OSEC-2026-03)**: `.install` files with destination filepaths attempting path escape via `..` or absolute paths are now invalidated ([ocaml/opam#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897))
- **Nix depexts** now loads package names from a JSON file instead of inlining them, fixing a potential string injection vulnerability ([ocaml/opam#6894](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6894))
- **Gentoo distribution detection restored** with support for single-quoted values in `/etc/os-release` ([ocaml/opam#6886](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6886))

**[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**
- **OxCaml support** merged across 140 files ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), adding Float32 operations, small integer types (`int8_u`, `int16_u`), unboxed int indexing, Float16 conversion primitives, and new atomic/domain primitives, by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- **JSPI effects availability** clarified in documentation: `--effects=jspi` works out-of-the-box in Chrome 137 and Node.js 25+ ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Six of ten repositories saw activity this week; [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) had no commits or PRs
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) shipped opam 2.5.1 ([ocaml/opam#6899](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6899)), a security and bug-fix release addressing a path-escape vulnerability (OSEC-2026-03) and two GC corruption bugs in C stubs
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) is approaching the 3.23.0 release ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)), targeting 2026-04-20, with 228 files changed across the week and 11 new features landing
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged OxCaml compatibility across 140 files ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), adding Float32, small int types, atomic primitives, and dedicated CI pipelines
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) merged three PRs bringing OxCaml upstream support ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)), OCaml 5.5 compatibility ([ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406)), and polymorphic parameter fixes ([ocaml/odoc#1404](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1404))
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) had four new PRs opened covering menhirLib snapshot updates, `misc.ml` synchronization with upstream OCaml, and saved parts investigation ([ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058), [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059), [ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060))
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) had only an automated Nix flake lock update ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)) bumping the merlin input to the 2026-04-17 snapshot
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune infrastructure work (managed Fd.t, Repr.t expansion, RPC hardening) and opam post-release CI cleanup dominated ongoing activity alongside OxCaml tracking issue creation in odoc.
group_summaries.activity:
**[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**
- [Per-module inter-library dependency filtering](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14116) under intensive review by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb), targeting [ocaml/dune#4572](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/4572); a 40-70% null-build regression is being investigated, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposing memoizing library closure computation ([ocaml/dune#14256](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14256)) as mitigation
- [Port `Async_io` to `Lev` (libev)](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14243) opened by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) to replace the `select`-based I/O loop and lift the 1024 fd limit ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243))
- [Action runners with bubblewrap sandbox](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14240) draft opened by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) for beta testing ahead of 3.24 ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240))
- [`%{pkg:foo:lib}` pforms for package install layouts](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/pull/14200) opened by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter), fixing [ocaml/dune#3378](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/3378) and [ocaml/dune#14193](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14193)
- New `Fd.t` managed file descriptor type introduced in `Stdune` replacing bare `Unix.file_descr` across multiple PRs ([ocaml/dune#14202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14202), [ocaml/dune#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14205), [ocaml/dune#14206](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14206), [ocaml/dune#14207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14207), [ocaml/dune#14210](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14210), [ocaml/dune#14216](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14216))
- OxCaml CI Nix job added ([ocaml/dune#14248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14248)) and revdeps-dev-build workflow replaced with a Nix derivation ([ocaml/dune#14228](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14228)) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) to fix OxCaml trunk CI ([ocaml/dune#14214](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14214))
- dune 3.22.2 release completed ([ocaml/dune#14112](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14112)) and 3.23.0 release tracker opened ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)

**[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058) to update menhirLib's snapshotting to use `--dump-menhirLib` flag after copy rules began failing with 2026 menhir versions
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059) to synchronize `misc.ml` and `format_doc.ml` with upstream OCaml compiler structure across 30 files
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) investigating saved parts handling and browse tree blowup, motivated by [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)
- Version constraint updated from `5.6-504` to `5.7.0-504` in ocaml-lsp CI check, signaling preparation for Merlin 5.7.0

**[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**
- [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) opened tracking issue [ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411) with seven sub-issues covering remaining OxCaml rendering gaps: unboxed types ([ocaml/odoc#1413](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1413)/[#1414](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1414)), layouts ([ocaml/odoc#1415](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1415)), modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416)), modes ([ocaml/odoc#1417](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1417)), `@zero-alloc` annotation ([ocaml/odoc#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418)), and `include functor` ([ocaml/odoc#1419](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1419))
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened three follow-on OxCaml PRs: modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)), kind annotations ([ocaml/odoc#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410)), and unboxed named types ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), all awaiting rebase before merge

**[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**
- GC corruption fixed in `OpamStubs.uname` by registering roots for intermediate strings ([ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880), by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm))
- GC corruption fixed in `OpamStubs.enumRegistry` on Windows ([ocaml/opam#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882))
- Minimum OCaml build version raised from 4.08 to 4.11 ([ocaml/opam#6879](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6879))
- `result` type renamed to `solver_result` in `opamTypes` to avoid conflict with `Stdlib.result` ([ocaml/opam#6885](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6885))
- Discussion opened on standardising C stub allocation patterns to prevent future GC corruptions ([ocaml/opam#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883), with review feedback from [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27))
- PR opened to remove build directories earlier during package installation, addressing performance issue [ocaml/opam#5884](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5884) ([ocaml/opam#6906](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6906))

**[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**
- **Wasm toplevel function size reduction** pursued via [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) (constant expressions into global variables, halving PRT benchmark toplevel size) and draft [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) (loop extraction for engine tiering), by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- WASI runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)) and Wasm native effects ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189)) remain blocked on [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2177](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177) and [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613) pending a new dune release
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **OxCaml compatibility effort spans four repositories**: [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) merged OxCaml support ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)); [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged OxCaml support across 140 files ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)); [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) added OxCaml CI Nix jobs ([ocaml/dune#14248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14248)) and a Nix derivation for OxCaml trunk builds ([ocaml/dune#14228](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14228)); [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)'s saved parts investigation ([ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060)) was motivated by OxCaml-related upstream OCaml changes in [ocaml/ocaml#14241](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14241)
- **js_of_ocaml WASI and Wasm effects PRs are blocked on a dune fix**: [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189) are blocked waiting on [ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2177) and a new dune release
- **opam 2.5.1 release coordination with setup-ocaml**: the opam 2.5.1 release ([ocaml/opam#6899](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6899)) caused a downstream breakage reported in [ocaml/opam#6901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6901) when pre-built binaries were not immediately available; [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) linked the corresponding setup-ocaml fix
- **Merlin version tracking across ocaml-lsp**: the [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) flake.lock update bumped the `merlin5_3` input from the 2026-03-16 snapshot to 2026-04-17, reflecting [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)'s version bump toward 5.7.0
- **Dune opam file generation changes affect opam workflow**: the new explicit promotion requirement for opam files ([ocaml/dune#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108)) directly affects how projects use [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) to publish packages, with `dune promote` now required before `opam publish`
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **dune 3.23.0 release tracker** ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), targeting 2026-04-20; current blockers include sandboxed package dependency regression ([ocaml/dune#14213](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14213)) and OxCaml CI compatibility ([ocaml/dune#14214](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14214))
- **Sandboxed package deps regression** ([ocaml/dune#14213](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14213)) raised by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) as a blocker for 3.23.0; a reproduction case is being developed in [ocaml/dune#14189](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14189)
- **Allow arbitrary opam fields in `dune-project`** ([ocaml/dune#14197](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14197)) opened by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV); [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed `(opam_fields (depexts "..."))` syntax as a lighter-weight alternative to opam.template files
- **Dune Monitor long-term plan** ([ocaml/dune#14121](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14121)) by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) outlines making `dune monitor` a standalone binary using Lwt instead of Fiber, with Notty/Lwd rendering and RPC/trace-file observation
- **opam 2.5.1 release impact on setup-ocaml** ([ocaml/opam#6901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6901)): [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) reported setup-ocaml breakage when binaries were unavailable at release; [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) clarified that release binaries are for the install script, not setup-ocaml
- **Supply chain security for opam releases** ([ocaml/opam#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900)): [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) requested switching to GitHub immutable releases; [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) suggested combining this with GitHub artifact attestations
- **opam-installer dependency cycle** ([ocaml/opam#6907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6907)): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) raised that `opam-installer` depends on `opam-core` which depends on `jsonm`, making it hard to bootstrap `jsonm` without opam
- **Merlin saved parts and browse tree blowup** ([ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060)): [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) identified that mimicking the typechecker's saved-types behavior leads to unwanted nesting; [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) suggested opening a tracking ticket for the performance implications
- **`@inline` tag on module declarations** ([ocaml/odoc#1408](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1408)): [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) explained why the approach differs from inlined includes — inline modules require tracking during path/reference resolution — and closed the PR as needs further architectural work
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml compatibility is becoming a group-wide priority**: dedicated support landed in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), and [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#14248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14248)) this week, each with dedicated CI pipelines and test suites
- **Nix flake as the standard cross-compiler testing mechanism**: [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) replaced its revdeps-dev-build workflow with a full Nix derivation ([ocaml/dune#14228](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14228)); [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) auto-updates its flake.lock ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)); [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) bumped `cachix/install-nix-action` ([ocaml/merlin#2056](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2056))
- **Synchronization with upstream OCaml compiler**: [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) has multiple open PRs ([ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058), [ocaml/merlin#2059](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2059)) reducing historical drift with upstream; [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) is tracking OCaml 5.5 features; [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) is blocked on WASI and effects work pending a new dune release ([ocaml/dune#13613](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13613))
- **Security hardening at the release and runtime level**: opam 2.5.1 addressed OSEC-2026-03 ([ocaml/opam#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897)) and two GC corruption bugs ([ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880), [ocaml/opam#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882)); downstream supply chain concerns are driving discussions about immutable releases and artifact attestations ([ocaml/opam#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900))
- **Resource safety abstractions in dune**: systematic replacement of raw `Unix.file_descr` with a managed `Fd.t` type ([ocaml/dune#14202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14202) et al.) mirrors opam's work standardising C stub GC root registration ([ocaml/opam#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883)), reflecting a broader push toward eliminating resource-management bugs
- **Wasm backend optimisation focus in js_of_ocaml**: both [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) target the toplevel/init function as a recurring Wasm engine bottleneck, alongside JSPI standardization shifting documentation from experimental caveats to concrete browser version guidance ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Major oxcaml/oxcaml week: scannable axes, static evaluator, metaprogramming beta, functor inlining limits, and Merlin signature-help fixes.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Scannable axes separability, static evaluator, metaprogramming beta, functor inlining limits, -Ix flag, non_pointer caml_modify skip, name mangling.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Scannable axes: separability becomes a scannable axis** in a large type system refactoring by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) — merges `Pointerness` into an expanded `Separability` lattice, renames the base sort `Value` to `Scannable` internally, and redefines `immediate` as `scannable non_pointer` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))
- **First version of static evaluator** for layout polymorphism merged by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) — a lambda calculus evaluator for statically evaluating layout-polymorphic expressions; inter-module evaluation not yet supported ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512))
- **Runtime metaprogramming promoted from alpha to beta extension** by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr), reflecting increased maturity of the feature ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843))
- **Separate functor inlining size limits** added by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell), introducing `-flambda2-small-functor-size` and `-flambda2-large-functor-size` flags; large functors are now ineligible for speculative inlining unless annotated, and `inline available` attributes were added to Stdlib's `Hashtbl`, `Map`, and `Set` functors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5878](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5878))
- **New `-Ix <dir>` compiler flag** added by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) to mark include directories where CMX files are guaranteed always available, as infrastructure for future layout polymorphism error reporting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654))
- **`caml_modify` skipped for `non_pointer` types** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), improving codegen for types whose values are never GC-traced pointers; also bumps the mixed block version to v6 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831))
- **New reversible name mangling scheme** (`Structured_mangling` module) introduced by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist), and Samuel Hym — encodes arbitrary strings into output-safe identifiers with full reversibility, used for parameterized library symbols ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5099](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5099))
- **Patricia tree bit-packed integer representation** merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp), improving internal data structure efficiency in Flambda2 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5833](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5833))
- **Tick thread state reset after `fork()`** in child process by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark), reinitializing mutex and restarting the tick thread in child processes to maintain `Unix.fork()` support in multithreaded programs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829))
- **Graceful error reporting** when runtime metaprogramming encounters unsupported language features, by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian), replacing internal errors with user-facing messages ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823))
- **Signature-help optional argument highlighting** fixed in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) so that already-provided labelled arguments are skipped and optional arguments are correctly surfaced as the active parameter ([oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216))
- **Signature-help trigger on unfinished `let...in` bindings** test case added in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) reproducing the bug where signature-help failed when the `in` keyword was missing ([oxcaml/merlin#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) was the dominant source of activity this week, with 13 significant features merged and numerous PRs opened across the compiler, runtime, Flambda2, and type system
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) merged two signature-help improvements and opened three new PRs for compiler sync, typeopt cleanup, and syntax-doc support for scannable axes
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) had two new CI-fix PRs from [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) and continued discussion on the patching infrastructure draft ([oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27))
- [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) saw a long-standing arm64 async_ssl patch PR ([janestreet/opam-repository#37](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/37)) closed without merging after 14 months
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) had no activity this week
- Approximately 24 contributors were active across the group, with the largest concentration in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)
group_summaries.activity_summary: 5.4 merge fixing, Reaper refactoring, register allocator improvements, layout polymorphism infrastructure, and opam CI stability work all advanced.
group_summaries.activity:
- **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) is in an active test-fixing phase: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) opened five fix PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5880](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5880), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5881), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5883](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5883), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5867](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5867)) and [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened the minus-31 changes merge ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5853](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5853)); [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) has multiple runtime PRs open ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5845](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5845), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5846](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5846), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5847](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5847))
- **Flambda2 Reaper refactoring** saw three cleanup PRs merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): dep_solver split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)), post-split cleanup ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)), and review address ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)); code size computation PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) remains open
- **IRC register allocator refinements** from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): merged split-pass occur check fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5537)), opened affinity-aware coalescing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5863](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5863)) and affinity data structure refactoring ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5849](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5849))
- **Layout polymorphism infrastructure**: [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) merged four PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5791](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5791), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792)); [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) opened experimental weak parameterized symbols ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5873](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5873)); [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) opened draft layout polymorphic functions PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870))
- **Flambda2 precision improvements** opened: improved alloc_mode use for unknown types ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5820](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5820)) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell), continuation argument type refinement ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5850](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5850)) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs), and improved `argument_types_useful` check ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5760](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5760)) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- **Testsuite**: [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) merged the large mlexamples migration into the main testsuite ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5346), 12k additions/42k deletions) and opened fexpr output checking for toplevel expect tests ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5837](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5837))
- **opam-repository CI fixes**: [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened [oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) cherry-picking fixes from the dev branch to repair builds of odoc, sherlodoc, ocaml-lsp-server, and macOS-specific dependencies
- **Merlin compiler sync and diff reduction**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened PRs to sync compiler changes from `5.2.0minus-37` ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234)) and reduce divergence in `typeopt.ml` ([oxcaml/merlin#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233)); also opened syntax-doc support for scannable axes ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
- **Various bug fixes merged in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: naked int8/int16 kind errors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5852](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5852)), consistent nullability in typeopt ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5841](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5841)), staging in Printtyp ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5785](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5785)), macOS compilation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5714](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5714)), REPL metaprogramming syntax directive ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801))
- **`additional union_total` function families** for patricia trees added by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs), avoiding allocations in common union operations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5803](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5803))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin syncing the `-Ix` compiler flag**: [oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) picks up the `5.2.0minus-37` diff which includes parsing of the new `-Ix` flag introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654)
- **Merlin syntax-doc support for scannable axes**: [oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235) tracks the type system changes from [oxcaml/oxcaml#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169), propagating the scannable axes redesign into Merlin's syntax documentation feature
- **Merlin subtree integration in oxcaml/oxcaml**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened an update to the Merlin import script to pull from the local workspace ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5872](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5872)) alongside the larger PR adding Merlin as a subtree ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568)), establishing a tighter coupling between the two repositories
- **[Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) active across both opam repositories**: opened CI-fix PRs in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) ([oxcaml/opam-repository#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37), [oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38)) and had a long-standing arm64 async_ssl PR ([janestreet/opam-repository#37](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository/issues/37)) closed in [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) the same week
- **[David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) active across repositories**: leading 5.4 merge fix PRs in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) while his patching infrastructure draft ([oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27)) continues in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)
- **oxcaml/opam-repository CI fix references upstream**: [oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) pins `checkseum` tracking an upstream fix at [ocaml/opam-repository#29747](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29747)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Scannable axes on abstract kinds** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874)) prompted a design discussion between [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) about where to place `Scannable_axes.t` in the kind representation — whether it belongs on `base_and_axes` or within each kind constructor — with consensus that the current approach is a reasonable interim pending a fuller kind system redesign
- **Diamond-aware mode error hints** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858)) generated discussion between [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) on correctly selecting which branch of a Meet/Join hint is "at fault" in diamond-shaped mode lattices, settling on using left-adjoint preservation of colimits
- **Alloc mode for returns** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5877](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5877)) received extensive early review from [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) identifying numerous places where `alloc_mode` should be changed to `return_mode`, particularly around `Lsend`, `Fexpr.alloc_mode_for_applications`, and `Cmx_format.alloc_mode`
- **Flambda2 CMX export info optimization** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5809](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5809)) by [Nicolas Trangez](https://github.com/NicolasT) proposed moving export data out of the inline Marshal block into a `.cmx` section to speed up linking, with [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) looping in [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) for awareness
- **External contributor overhead** surfaced when [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) replaced an external contributor's portability fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5848](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5848) by [AmberDevs](https://github.com/amber181)) with an identical internal PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5865](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5865)) due to administrative overhead for external contributions
- **`layout.sh` placement** debated in [oxcaml/opam-repository#27](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/27): [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) asked whether the patch-guard generation script should live in internal release infrastructure; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) argued for keeping it public to ease external contributor access
- **Merlin signature-help PR coordination** in [oxcaml/merlin#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223): [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) is holding review until [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) rebases after [oxcaml/merlin#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215) and [oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) were merged
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Flambda2 Reaper** (dead code elimination) is receiving intensive refactoring across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): `dep_solver.ml` was split into separate points-to analysis, unboxing analysis, and types rewrite modules ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)), with code size computation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) still pending
- **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** is in an active test-fixing phase in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) with many small PRs resolving individual test suite failures across typing, runtime, and effects subsystems; the merge branch consumed significant contributor bandwidth this week
- **Layout polymorphism** infrastructure is expanding in multiple directions: static evaluation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512)), persistent module staticity ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5834](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5834)), CMI serialization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835)), draft layout polymorphic functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870)), and experimental weak symbol deduplication ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5873](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5873))
- **Runtime metaprogramming** is progressing through staged rollout in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): promoted from alpha to beta ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843)) with improved error reporting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823)) and REPL compatibility fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801))
- **Patricia tree optimizations** continue to land incrementally in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml): bit-packed integer representation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5833](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5833)), allocation-avoiding `union_total` families ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5803](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5803)), and a pending high-bits flag proposal ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5543](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5543))
- **IRC register allocator** receiving a series of refinements in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): affinity-aware coalescing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5863](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5863)), split-pass occur check ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5537)), and structural refactoring ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5849](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5849))
- **Merlin compiler diff reduction** is an active maintenance theme: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) is systematically syncing upstream compiler changes ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234)) and reducing per-file divergence ([oxcaml/merlin#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233)) as Merlin moves toward subtree integration ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568))
- **opam overlay CI stability** remains an active concern in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository): both [oxcaml/opam-repository#37](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/37) and [oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) address build failures across odoc, sherlodoc, ocaml-lsp-server, and macOS dependencies

group_summaries.brief_summary: Typechecker improvements, stdlib additions (Atomic.update, pp_print_dynarray), runtime fixes, and ephemeron GC refactoring in ocaml/ocaml.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Format.pp_print_dynarray added, nested with-constraints in package types supported, and Atomic.update merged.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** added to the standard library for printing `Dynarray.t` values with a separator, mirroring `pp_print_array` and `pp_print_iarray` — [ocaml/ocaml#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726)
- **Nested `with` constraints in package types** now supported as syntactic sugar: `(module T with type x = ... with type y = ...)` accepted alongside the existing `and` form — [ocaml/ocaml#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14710), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14274](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14274)
- **`Atomic.update`** (formerly `Atomic.modify`) merged, providing a high-level retry-loop abstraction over `compare_and_set` — [ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798)
- **`ctype.mli`** exposes new `filter_arity` function and `Pattern_env.save`/`reset` state for external use — [ocaml/ocaml#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738)
- **`errortrace.mli`** gains a new `Kind_differ` error variant for better reporting of mismatched privacy levels on object method types — [ocaml/ocaml#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity concentrated in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml); [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no new activity this week
- 12 PRs merged in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) spanning typechecker correctness, memory optimization, runtime fixes, and build system improvements
- 7 new PRs/issues opened across stdlib, runtime, and documentation areas, including multiple competing implementations for [ocaml/ocaml#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658) (OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffixes)
- 14 individual contributors active in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) this week, led by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Twelve PRs merged covering typechecker correctness, memory optimizations, runtime bug fixes, and build system improvements.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Merged** — [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) fixed a spurious syntactic arity error for module-dependent arrow types — [ocaml/ocaml#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14719](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14719)
- **Merged** — [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) reduced typechecker memory use during exhaustiveness counter-example search by scoping type node pools to individual branches — [ocaml/ocaml#14727](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14727)
- **Merged** — [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) simplified field-kind handling in `eqtype` and `moregen` — [ocaml/ocaml#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738) — and unified the order of missing-field checks between the two functions — [ocaml/ocaml#14737](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14737)
- **Merged** — [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) enabled `-Wwrite-strings` throughout the C runtime — [ocaml/ocaml#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666) — followed by a Cygwin build fix it exposed — [ocaml/ocaml#14751](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14751)
- **Merged** — [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) fixed an uninitialized `malloc` in `ocamlyacc`'s `symtab.c` that could produce incorrect line directives — [ocaml/ocaml#14748](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14748), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14747](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14747)
- **Merged** — [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) added overflow checking to `caml_ba_reshape` fixing an out-of-bounds read/segfault — [ocaml/ocaml#14691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14691), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14655) and [ocaml/ocaml#14674](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14674)
- **Merged** — [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) hardened `ocamltest` to reject expect tests that use `include` and fixed AFL test noise — [ocaml/ocaml#14749](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14749)
- **Merged** — [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixed swapped `None`/`Some` branches in `bytecomp/dll.ml` `open_dll` when upgrading a DLL from checking to execution mode — [ocaml/ocaml#14703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14703)
- **Merged** — [Brandon Stride](https://github.com/brandonzstride) added a regression test for the `rectypes` keep-expansion fix — [ocaml/ocaml#14730](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14730), covers [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/11648)
- **Newly opened** — [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754) to fix cyclic types introduced by `with type` module constraints by rechecking well-foundedness after applying them
- **Newly opened** — [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746) to allow aliases when checking signature inclusion, fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14204](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14204)
- **Newly opened** — [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14759](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14759) to add C stubs for testing `OCAMLRUNPARAM` parsing directly
- **Newly opened** — [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757) to add explicit unit suffixes to memory-size OCAMLRUNPARAM options, and [ocaml/ocaml#14755](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14755) to fix a broken link in `testsuite/HACKING.adoc`
- **Newly opened** — [Yihang "Corvo" Liu](https://github.com/glyh) opened [ocaml/ocaml#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756) adding `kw/Mw/Gw`, `kB/MB/GB`, and `kiB/MiB/GiB` suffixes to OCAMLRUNPARAM word-sized params, closing [ocaml/ocaml#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658)
- **Newly opened (bug)** — [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) reported an internal error in `select_win32.c` related to cross-list socket merging corrupting `lpOrigIdx` — [ocaml/ocaml#14758](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14758)
- **Closed without merge** — [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) closed [ocaml/ocaml#14736](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14736) after discovering it incorrectly accepted parameter-reordered types; superseded by [ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffix design** attracted discussion between [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan), and [Yihang "Corvo" Liu](https://github.com/glyh) about whether to support `MiB`/`MB`/`Mw` or only IEC-style prefixes to avoid SI vs binary ambiguity — [ocaml/ocaml#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757), [ocaml/ocaml#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756), [ocaml/ocaml#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658)
- **`ocamlyacc` `calloc` vs `malloc`** prompted an exchange between [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) about whether zero-initialization by default hides bugs or reduces CVE risk in this legacy codebase — [ocaml/ocaml#14748](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14748)
- **Merging `eqtype` and `moregen`** continued with [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) landing two preparatory PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738), [ocaml/ocaml#14737](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14737)) and opening [ocaml/ocaml#14739](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14739) for the final merge; [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) noted subtle differences in variable handling — [ocaml/ocaml#14364](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14364)
- **Mass typo-fix PR policy** sparked debate when [ocaml/ocaml#14752](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14752) was submitted fixing `occured` → `occurred` across `ocamldoc`; [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) expressed concern about accepting PRs optimising GitHub metrics, with [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) ultimately merging and closing the discussion
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Ephemeron GC refactoring** is a multi-PR campaign by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) building on [ocaml/ocaml#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720): [ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) refactors phase-change helpers, [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734) fixes orphaning during sweep phase, [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733) clarifies ephemeron counters, and [ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735) introduces higher-level list operations
- **C compiler warnings cleanup** is an ongoing effort by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): `-Wwrite-strings` landed this week ([ocaml/ocaml#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666)) and `-Wformat-signedness` ([ocaml/ocaml#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694)) is open for review
- **Typechecker diagnostics improvements** feature prominently this week: object-type privacy errors ([ocaml/ocaml#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738)), signature-inclusion alias errors ([ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)), and arity error fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721)) all landed or are in review
- **`Atomic` stdlib expansion**: `Atomic.update` merged ([ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798)) and `Atomic.fold_update` ([ocaml/ocaml#14725](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14725)) is proposed as a follow-up to support pop-style operations on lock-free data structures

group_summaries.brief_summary: Low-activity week; eio saw a Windows backend CPU spin bug identified and an unlink PR awaiting review.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was limited to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio); all other repositories (domainslib, ocaml-uring, meio, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) had no recorded activity
