- Two open threads in eio: a pending PR for `Eio.Path.unlink` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833)) and a newly filed Windows backend bug ([ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837))
group_summaries.activity_summary: In eio, an unlink PR awaits review feedback and a Windows high-CPU bug was filed with root cause identified.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) followed up on [ocaml-multicore/eio#833](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/833), which adds a `missing_ok` parameter to `Eio.Path.unlink` (tracking the underlying request in [ocaml-multicore/eio#828](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/828)), asking [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) for guidance on what changes are needed for the PR to be accepted
- [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) filed [ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) reporting 100% CPU usage on Windows with the `eio_windows` backend when using `Fiber.both` and `Eio.Flow.copy` for bidirectional TCP proxy workloads; the same pattern works correctly on Linux
group_summaries.notable_discussions: - In [ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837), [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) traced the Windows CPU spin to `lib/eio_windows/sched.ml`: the `update` function does not remove a file descriptor from `to_write` when transitioning to read-only mode (and vice versa), causing both read and write events to be polled simultaneously and spinning the scheduler; a locally patched version confirmed the fix resolves the issue
group_summaries.emerging_trends: - Windows backend correctness in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) is surfacing as an area needing attention: [ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) exposes a latent poll-set bookkeeping bug in `eio_windows` that causes busy-looping under real-world bidirectional I/O workloads, suggesting Windows-specific code paths may benefit from more systematic review and testing

weekly_summary.brief_summary: opam 2.5.1 security release, OxCaml gains scannable axes and metaprogramming beta, dune targets 3.23.0, and odoc/js_of_ocaml add OxCaml support.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: opam 2.5.1 security fix, OxCaml scannable axes/static evaluator/metaprogramming beta, dune compile_commands.json and SIGTERM, odoc/js_of_ocaml OxCaml support.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam 2.5.1 released** ([ocaml/opam#6899](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6899)) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), addressing security vulnerability **OSEC-2026-03** — `.install` files with path-escape attempts via `..` or absolute paths are now invalidated ([ocaml/opam#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897)); Nix depexts JSON injection fix ([ocaml/opam#6894](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6894)); Gentoo detection restored ([ocaml/opam#6886](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6886))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Scannable axes: separability becomes a scannable axis** in a large type system refactoring by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) — merges `Pointerness` into an expanded `Separability` lattice and redefines `immediate` as `scannable non_pointer` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **First version of static evaluator** for layout polymorphism merged by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) — a lambda calculus evaluator for statically evaluating layout-polymorphic expressions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Runtime metaprogramming promoted from alpha to beta extension** by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843)), with improved graceful error reporting for unsupported features ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Separate functor inlining size limits** added by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell), introducing `-flambda2-small-functor-size` and `-flambda2-large-functor-size` flags; `inline available` attributes added to Stdlib's `Hashtbl`, `Map`, and `Set` functors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5878](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5878))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **New `-Ix <dir>` compiler flag** added by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) to mark include directories where CMX files are always available, as infrastructure for future layout polymorphism error reporting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`caml_modify` skipped for `non_pointer` types** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), improving codegen for types never GC-traced; bumps mixed block version to v6 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Tick thread state reset after `fork()`** in child process by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark), reinitializing mutex and restarting the tick thread to maintain `Unix.fork()` support in multithreaded programs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Signature-help optional argument highlighting** fixed in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) so already-provided labelled arguments are skipped and optional arguments are correctly surfaced ([oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **compile_commands.json generation** for C/C++ foreign stubs in dune, produced as part of `@check` under `(lang dune 3.23)`, enabling clangd integration ([ocaml/dune#14185](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14185), by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **SIGTERM before SIGKILL** sent to child processes on shutdown and watch-mode cancellation, closing a 7-year-old issue ([ocaml/dune#14224](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14224), fixes [ocaml/dune#2445](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/2445), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Opam file generation now requires explicit promotion** via `dune promote` under dune 3.23; source opam files used directly in release mode ([ocaml/dune#14108](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14108), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Inline tests execute for byte-only libraries** when a library has `(modes byte)` ([ocaml/dune#14174](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14174), fixes [ocaml/dune#9757](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9757), by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml upstream support merged into odoc** across modes, layouts, iarray, labeled tuples, and unboxed records ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399), via [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)); **OCaml 5.5 compatibility** added including external types and module arrow types ([ocaml/odoc#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406), by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml support merged into js_of_ocaml** across 140 files ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), adding Float32 operations, small integer types (`int8_u`, `int16_u`), unboxed int indexing, Float16 conversion primitives, and atomic/domain primitives, by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Format.pp_print_dynarray`** added for printing `Dynarray.t` values with a separator, mirroring `pp_print_array` and `pp_print_iarray` ([ocaml/ocaml#14726](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14726))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Nested `with` constraints in package types** now supported as syntactic sugar: `(module T with type x = ... with type y = ...)` accepted alongside the existing `and` form ([ocaml/ocaml#14710](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14710), closes [ocaml/ocaml#14274](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14274))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Atomic.update`** (formerly `Atomic.modify`) merged, providing a high-level retry-loop abstraction over `compare_and_set` ([ocaml/ocaml#10798](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/10798))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ocgtk 0.1~preview1** adds OCaml bindings for GTK 4 and a new `conf-gtk4` virtual package with depexts for Linux and macOS ([ocaml/opam-repository#29729](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29729))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ppx_mixins 0.1** introduces a PPX for mixing module type signatures including `[%mixins Printable + Comparable]` syntax ([ocaml/opam-repository#29741](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29741), by [Sacha Ayoun](https://github.com/giltho))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **ocaml-ai-sdk 0.2** adds stream transformers, retry with exponential backoff, OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry, and Unicode-aware chunking ([ocaml/opam-repository#29724](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29724))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **openrouter_api 0.0.1** is a new Async-based client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API covering chat completions with streaming, tools, and structured outputs ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **atd 4.1.0** (13 packages) adds `atd-jsonlike` and `atd-yamlx` packages plus `<json repr="object">` for externally-tagged sum types ([ocaml/opam-repository#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709), by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Hilite MDX syntax highlighting** rolled out consistently across all ocaml.org content parsers, fixing long-standing incorrect highlighting of OCaml interactive session syntax ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Platform release scraper** now auto-detects pre-releases and supports per-project ignore patterns; `alpha`/`beta`/`rc[0-9]`/`preview` tags automatically marked `experimental: true` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3616))

-**ocaml-ai-sdk 0.2** adds stream transformers, retry with exponential backoff, OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry, and Unicode-aware chunking ([ocaml/opam-repository#29724](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29724))

-**openrouter_api 0.0.1** is a new Async-based client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API covering chat completions with streaming, tools, and structured outputs ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745))

-**atd 4.1.0** (13 packages) adds `atd-jsonlike` and `atd-yamlx` packages plus `<json repr="object">` for externally-tagged sum types ([ocaml/opam-repository#29709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29709), by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon))

-**Hilite MDX syntax highlighting** rolled out consistently across all ocaml.org content parsers, fixing long-standing incorrect highlighting of OCaml interactive session syntax ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3593))

-**Platform release scraper** now auto-detects pre-releases and supports per-project ignore patterns; `alpha`/`beta`/`rc[0-9]`/`preview` tags automatically marked `experimental: true` ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3616))
oxcaml: -**Scannable axes: separability becomes a scannable axis** in a large type system refactoring by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) — merges `Pointerness` into an expanded `Separability` lattice and redefines `immediate` as `scannable non_pointer` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169))

-**First version of static evaluator** for layout polymorphism merged by [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) — a lambda calculus evaluator for statically evaluating layout-polymorphic expressions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512))

-**Runtime metaprogramming promoted from alpha to beta extension** by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843)), with improved graceful error reporting for unsupported features ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823))

-**Separate functor inlining size limits** added by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell), introducing `-flambda2-small-functor-size` and `-flambda2-large-functor-size` flags; `inline available` attributes added to Stdlib's `Hashtbl`, `Map`, and `Set` functors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5878](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5878))

-**New `-Ix <dir>` compiler flag** added by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) to mark include directories where CMX files are always available, as infrastructure for future layout polymorphism error reporting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5654](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5654))

-**`caml_modify` skipped for `non_pointer` types** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa), improving codegen for types never GC-traced; bumps mixed block version to v6 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5831](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5831))

-**Tick thread state reset after `fork()`** in child process by [@glittershark](https://github.com/glittershark), reinitializing mutex and restarting the tick thread to maintain `Unix.fork()` support in multithreaded programs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829))

-**Signature-help optional argument highlighting** fixed in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) so already-provided labelled arguments are skipped and optional arguments are correctly surfaced ([oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam 2.5.1 released** as a security and bug-fix release by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate), addressing path-escape vulnerability OSEC-2026-03 ([ocaml/opam#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897)) and two GC corruption bugs in C stubs ([ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880), [ocaml/opam#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune 3.23.0** is targeting release on 2026-04-20 ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) with compile_commands.json generation, SIGTERM-before-SIGKILL on process shutdown, opam file explicit promotion, and a new managed `Fd.t` type replacing bare `Unix.file_descr` across 6 PRs
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) merged 13 significant features this week spanning the type system (scannable axes [oxcaml/oxcaml#5169](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5169)), optimizer (static evaluator [oxcaml/oxcaml#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512), functor inlining limits [oxcaml/oxcaml#5878](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5878)), runtime (tick thread fork safety [oxcaml/oxcaml#5829](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5829)), and tooling (metaprogramming beta [oxcaml/oxcaml#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml compatibility** landed in both [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)) this week, reflecting a coordinated push to align tooling with the OxCaml compiler
- __RUMINANT:core__ 12 PRs merged in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) covering typechecker correctness, memory optimization, runtime bug fixes, and stdlib additions; 14 contributors active led by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), and [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA)

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Flambda2 Reaper refactoring, IRC register allocator refinements, dune Fd.t managed descriptors, layout polymorphism infrastructure, and OxCaml opam CI stability all progressed.
weekly_summary.activity:
**Core ([ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml))**
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) fixed a spurious syntactic arity error for module-dependent arrow types ([ocaml/ocaml#14721](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14721)) and reduced typechecker memory use during exhaustiveness counter-example search by scoping type node pools to individual branches ([ocaml/ocaml#14727](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14727))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) simplified field-kind handling in `eqtype` and `moregen` ([ocaml/ocaml#14738](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14738)) and unified the order of missing-field checks between the two functions ([ocaml/ocaml#14737](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14737)), continuing the multi-week eqtype/moregen unification campaign ([ocaml/ocaml#14364](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14364))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) enabled `-Wwrite-strings` throughout the C runtime ([ocaml/ocaml#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666)) followed by a Cygwin build fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14751](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14751)); **overflow checking** added to `caml_ba_reshape` by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan), fixing an out-of-bounds read/segfault ([ocaml/ocaml#14691](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14691))
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) fixed an uninitialized `malloc` in `ocamlyacc`'s `symtab.c` that could produce incorrect line directives ([ocaml/ocaml#14748](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14748)); [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) fixed swapped `None`/`Some` branches in `bytecomp/dll.ml` ([ocaml/ocaml#14703](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14703))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Ephemeron GC refactoring** series by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) continues: phase-change helpers refactored ([ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732)), sweep-phase orphaning fixed ([ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734)), counter semantics clarified ([ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733)), higher-level list operations introduced ([ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735))
- __RUMINANT:core__ New PRs opened: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened fixes for cyclic types from `with type` constraints ([ocaml/ocaml#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754)) and aliases in signature inclusion ([ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)); multiple competing implementations of OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffixes opened ([ocaml/ocaml#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757), [ocaml/ocaml#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756))

**OxCaml ([oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml))**
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** is in an active test-fixing phase: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened five fix PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5880](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5880), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5881), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5883](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5883), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5867](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5867)); [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) has multiple runtime PRs open ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5845](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5845), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5846](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5846), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5847](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5847))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Flambda2 Reaper refactoring** saw three PRs merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): dep_solver split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)), post-split cleanup ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)), and review address ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)); code size computation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) remains open
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **IRC register allocator** refinements from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): split-pass occur check fix merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5537)), affinity-aware coalescing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5863](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5863)) and affinity data structure refactoring ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5849](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5849)) opened
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Layout polymorphism infrastructure**: [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) merged four PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5791](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5791), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792)); draft layout polymorphic functions PR opened ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Testsuite**: [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) merged the large mlexamples migration into the main testsuite ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5346), 12k additions/42k deletions)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin compiler sync and diff reduction**: [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened PRs to sync compiler changes from `5.2.0minus-37` ([oxcaml/merlin#234](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/234)), reduce divergence in `typeopt.ml` ([oxcaml/merlin#233](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/233)), and add syntax-doc support for scannable axes ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **opam-repository CI fixes**: [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened [oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) cherry-picking fixes to repair builds of odoc, sherlodoc, ocaml-lsp-server, and macOS-specific dependencies

**Tools**
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Per-module inter-library dependency filtering ([ocaml/dune#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14116)) under intensive review by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb), investigating a 40-70% null-build regression; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed memoizing library closure computation as mitigation ([ocaml/dune#14256](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14256))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ New `Fd.t` managed file descriptor type introduced in `Stdune` replacing bare `Unix.file_descr` across 6 PRs ([ocaml/dune#14202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14202), [ocaml/dune#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14205), [ocaml/dune#14206](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14206), [ocaml/dune#14207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14207), [ocaml/dune#14210](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14210), [ocaml/dune#14216](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14216))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) opened a draft for porting `Async_io` to `Lev` (libev) to replace the `select`-based I/O loop and lift the 1024 fd limit ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243)), and a bubblewrap sandbox action runner for beta testing ahead of 3.24 ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058) to update menhirLib snapshotting after copy rules began failing with 2026 menhir versions, and [ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) investigating saved parts handling and browse tree blowup
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **odoc OxCaml tracking issue** opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) covering seven remaining rendering gaps: unboxed types, layouts, modalities, modes, `@zero-alloc`, and `include functor`; follow-on PRs opened by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) for modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) and unboxed named types ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ GC corruptions fixed in opam: `OpamStubs.uname` C stub by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880)) and Windows `enumRegistry` ([ocaml/opam#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882)); discussion opened on standardising C stub allocation patterns ([ocaml/opam#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm toplevel function size reduction** pursued via constant expressions into global variables ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211), halving PRT benchmark toplevel size) and loop extraction for engine tiering ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)), both by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)

-New PRs opened: [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel) opened fixes for cyclic types from `with type` constraints ([ocaml/ocaml#14754](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14754)) and aliases in signature inclusion ([ocaml/ocaml#14746](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14746)); multiple competing implementations of OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffixes opened ([ocaml/ocaml#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757), [ocaml/ocaml#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756))
**OxCaml ([oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml))**
tools: -Per-module inter-library dependency filtering ([ocaml/dune#14116](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14116)) under intensive review by [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb), investigating a 40-70% null-build regression; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) proposed memoizing library closure computation as mitigation ([ocaml/dune#14256](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14256))

-New `Fd.t` managed file descriptor type introduced in `Stdune` replacing bare `Unix.file_descr` across 6 PRs ([ocaml/dune#14202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14202), [ocaml/dune#14205](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14205), [ocaml/dune#14206](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14206), [ocaml/dune#14207](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14207), [ocaml/dune#14210](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14210), [ocaml/dune#14216](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14216))

-[Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) opened a draft for porting `Async_io` to `Lev` (libev) to replace the `select`-based I/O loop and lift the 1024 fd limit ([ocaml/dune#14243](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14243)), and a bubblewrap sandbox action runner for beta testing ahead of 3.24 ([ocaml/dune#14240](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14240))

-[Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) opened [ocaml/merlin#2058](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2058) to update menhirLib snapshotting after copy rules began failing with 2026 menhir versions, and [ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060) investigating saved parts handling and browse tree blowup

-**odoc OxCaml tracking issue** opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) ([ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) covering seven remaining rendering gaps: unboxed types, layouts, modalities, modes, `@zero-alloc`, and `include functor`; follow-on PRs opened by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) for modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) and unboxed named types ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407))

-GC corruptions fixed in opam: `OpamStubs.uname` C stub by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml/opam#6880](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6880)) and Windows `enumRegistry` ([ocaml/opam#6882](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6882)); discussion opened on standardising C stub allocation patterns ([ocaml/opam#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883))

-**Wasm toplevel function size reduction** pursued via constant expressions into global variables ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211), halving PRT benchmark toplevel size) and loop extraction for engine tiering ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)), both by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)

ecosystem: -**dune 3.22.2** (18 packages) landed in [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29704](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29704)) fixing a `--diff-command` regression; changelog added to ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3608))

-**Backstage posts** published on wasm_of_ocaml ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3612](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3612)) and Merlin signature help ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3610)) by [sabine](https://github.com/sabine)
oxcaml: -**OCaml 5.4 upstream merge** is in an active test-fixing phase: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened five fix PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5880](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5880), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5881), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5882](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5882), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5883](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5883), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5867](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5867)); [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) has multiple runtime PRs open ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5845](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5845), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5846](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5846), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5847](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5847))

-**Flambda2 Reaper refactoring** saw three PRs merged by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs): dep_solver split ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776)), post-split cleanup ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787)), and review address ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)); code size computation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) remains open

-**IRC register allocator** refinements from [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc): split-pass occur check fix merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5537](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5537)), affinity-aware coalescing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5863](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5863)) and affinity data structure refactoring ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5849](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5849)) opened

-**Layout polymorphism infrastructure**: [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) merged four PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5791](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5791), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5817](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5817), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5792](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5792)); draft layout polymorphic functions PR opened ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870))

-**Testsuite**: [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) merged the large mlexamples migration into the main testsuite ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5346](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5346), 12k additions/42k deletions)

-**opam-repository CI fixes**: [Matthew Else](https://github.com/matthewelse) opened [oxcaml/opam-repository#38](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/38) cherry-picking fixes to repair builds of odoc, sherlodoc, ocaml-lsp-server, and macOS-specific dependencies

multicore: -In [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio), [张睿 Zhang Rui](https://github.com/z-rui) filed [ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837) reporting 100% CPU usage on Windows with `eio_windows` under bidirectional TCP proxy workloads; root cause traced to `sched.ml`'s `update` function not removing fds from `to_write` when transitioning to read-only mode

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **OCAMLRUNPARAM unit suffix design** attracted discussion between [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Yang Lin](https://github.com/y1lan), and [Yihang "Corvo" Liu](https://github.com/glyh) about whether to support `MiB`/`MB`/`Mw` or only IEC-style prefixes to avoid SI vs binary ambiguity ([ocaml/ocaml#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14757), [ocaml/ocaml#14756](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14756), [ocaml/ocaml#14658](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14658))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Merging `eqtype` and `moregen`** continued with [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) landing two preparatory PRs and opening [ocaml/ocaml#14739](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14739) for the final merge; [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) noted subtle differences in variable handling ([ocaml/ocaml#14364](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14364))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`ocamlyacc` `calloc` vs `malloc`** prompted an exchange between [Xavier Leroy](https://github.com/xavierleroy), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) about whether zero-initialization by default hides bugs or reduces CVE risk in this legacy codebase ([ocaml/ocaml#14748](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14748))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Scannable axes on abstract kinds** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874)) prompted a design discussion between [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) about where to place `Scannable_axes.t` in the kind representation, with consensus that the current approach is a reasonable interim pending a fuller kind system redesign
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Diamond-aware mode error hints** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858)) generated discussion between [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) on correctly selecting which branch of a Meet/Join hint is "at fault" in diamond-shaped mode lattices, settling on using left-adjoint preservation of colimits
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **External contributor overhead** surfaced when [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) replaced an external contributor's portability fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5848](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5848) by [AmberDevs](https://github.com/amber181)) with an identical internal PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5865](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5865)) due to administrative overhead for external contributions
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **dune 3.23.0 release tracker** ([ocaml/dune#14211](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14211)) opened by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), targeting 2026-04-20; current blockers include sandboxed package dependency regression ([ocaml/dune#14213](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14213)) and OxCaml CI compatibility ([ocaml/dune#14214](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14214))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Supply chain security for opam releases** ([ocaml/opam#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900)): [Austin Theriault](https://github.com/ajbt200128) requested switching to GitHub immutable releases; [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) suggested combining this with GitHub artifact attestations
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam-installer dependency cycle** ([ocaml/opam#6907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6907)): [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) raised that `opam-installer` depends on `opam-core` which depends on `jsonm`, making it hard to bootstrap `jsonm` without opam
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin saved parts and browse tree blowup** ([ocaml/merlin#2060](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2060)): [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) identified that mimicking the typechecker's saved-types behavior leads to unwanted nesting in the browse tree
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **MSYS2 UCRT64 transition** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579)) merged by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto): six `conf-mingw-w64-*-x86_64` packages now support UCRT64, prompted by MSYS2's deprecation of MINGW64 in March 2026
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Windows eio CPU spin root cause identified** in [ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837): the `update` function in `lib/eio_windows/sched.ml` does not remove an fd from `to_write` when transitioning to read-only mode, causing both read and write events to be polled simultaneously; a locally patched version confirmed the fix

oxcaml: -**Scannable axes on abstract kinds** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5874](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5874)) prompted a design discussion between [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) about where to place `Scannable_axes.t` in the kind representation, with consensus that the current approach is a reasonable interim pending a fuller kind system redesign

-**Diamond-aware mode error hints** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5858](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5858)) generated discussion between [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) and [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) on correctly selecting which branch of a Meet/Join hint is "at fault" in diamond-shaped mode lattices, settling on using left-adjoint preservation of colimits

-**External contributor overhead** surfaced when [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) replaced an external contributor's portability fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5848](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5848) by [@amber181](https://github.com/amber181)) with an identical internal PR ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5865](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5865)) due to administrative overhead for external contributions
multicore: -**Windows eio CPU spin root cause identified** in [ocaml-multicore/eio#837](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/837): the `update` function in `lib/eio_windows/sched.ml` does not remove an fd from `to_write` when transitioning to read-only mode, causing both read and write events to be polled simultaneously; a locally patched version confirmed the fix

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml compatibility is becoming a group-wide priority across tooling**: dedicated support landed in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1399](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1399)), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2105](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2105)), and [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#14248](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14248)) in the same week, each with dedicated CI pipelines; [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) is tracking new type system features ([oxcaml/merlin#235](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/235)) and moving toward subtree integration ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5568](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5568))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Layout polymorphism** infrastructure is expanding in multiple directions: static evaluation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5512](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5512)), CMI serialization ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5835](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5835)), draft layout polymorphic functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5870](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5870)), experimental weak symbol deduplication ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5873](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5873)), and now a static evaluator landing as the first step toward full inter-module evaluation
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Runtime metaprogramming** is progressing through staged rollout: promoted from alpha to beta ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5843](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5843)) with improved error reporting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5823](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5823)) and REPL compatibility fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5801](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5801)), reflecting increasing maturity of this feature
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Flambda2 Reaper** (dead code elimination) receiving intensive structural refactoring: `dep_solver.ml` split into separate points-to analysis, unboxing analysis, and types rewrite modules ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5776](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5776), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5787](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5787), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5875](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5875)) with code size computation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5793](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5793)) still pending
- __RUMINANT:core__ **C compiler warnings cleanup** is an ongoing effort by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): `-Wwrite-strings` landed ([ocaml/ocaml#14666](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14666)) and `-Wformat-signedness` ([ocaml/ocaml#14694](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14694)) is open for review, continuing a multi-week campaign to clean up the C runtime codebase
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Ephemeron GC hardening** is a structured multi-PR campaign by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) building on [ocaml/ocaml#14720](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14720); four active PRs ([ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732), [ocaml/ocaml#14733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14733), [ocaml/ocaml#14734](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14734), [ocaml/ocaml#14735](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14735)) systematically clarify phase-change logic for the multicore runtime
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Resource safety abstractions** are advancing in parallel across tools: dune's systematic replacement of raw `Unix.file_descr` with a managed `Fd.t` type ([ocaml/dune#14202](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14202) et al.) mirrors opam's work standardising C stub GC root registration ([ocaml/opam#6883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6883))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Security hardening at release and runtime level**: opam 2.5.1 addressed OSEC-2026-03 ([ocaml/opam#6897](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6897)) and two GC corruption bugs; downstream supply chain concerns are driving discussions about immutable releases and GitHub artifact attestations ([ocaml/opam#6900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6900))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **AI/LLM tooling** continues to grow: `ocaml-ai-sdk` 0.2 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29724](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29724)) adds streaming and OpenTelemetry telemetry, and `openrouter_api` 0.0.1 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29745](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29745)) provides access to multiple LLM providers, building on the 0.1 release from last week
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Windows/MSYS2 support improvements** accumulating: UCRT64 support for mingw-w64 conf packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29579](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29579)), MSYS2 depexts for additional conf packages, and multiple compatibility fixes reflecting sustained investment in Windows OCaml support
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm backend optimisation focus in js_of_ocaml**: both [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212) target the toplevel/init function as a recurring Wasm engine bottleneck; JSPI documentation updated to reflect native availability in Chrome 137 and Node.js 25+ ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2213](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2213))
